ref: They begged him just to let them touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
text: Bálɛ wǝ lekpakpa bǝ lǝ utǝ lǝ befimfi lǝ betidi wǝ awu eto atosi ete. Nya mba kenke dítidi wǝ awu eto atosii, mǝ bufi ǝyuǝ mǝ.

ref: Jesus answered, “Every plant that my Father in heaven did not plant will be pulled up by the roots.
text: Yesu ǝtǝ mǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Kuyi saa kuyi ko mɔ Anto wǝ nkpe Kato dímantoo, boosu ko bewi.

text: “Beso fɛ fǝ bakasebi leenyifo lǝ bo banamǝ eto akpa ǝsuǝ? Bǝǝmflǝ mǝ ani kukɔnɔ fe kase bo akpa laate fɛ beedi lesa!”

ref: Jesus answered: Why do you disobey God and follow your own teaching?
text: Yesu átɔ mǝ nkǝ, “Nya beso biani nya bienyifo lǝ Onanto eto afí ǝsuǝ fɛ biǝtǝkǝko mi batekoko, banakoko eto asatete kama?

text: Fɛɛ mi áte biǝ utidi nwǝ lesatsyuǝ nte nkǝ woofo ni bufi kawunsǝko wǝ anto nye ambe fɛ ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, ‘Mfi lesa nǝmfo ntsyǝ kɛnyɛ miǝ muufi ni ntǝ Onanto.’

ref: Is this any way to show respect to your parents? You ignore God's commands in order to follow your own teaching.
text: Ɛyɛ lǝ nyamfo ǝsuǝǝ, yensi fe bǝ bebu mǝ anto mǝ. Kusu komfo ǝsuǝ nya biayɛ kani Onanto eto kɔfi itǝ lǝ bitǝkǝko mi kosate eto asatete kama nɛ.

ref: It is useless for you to worship me, when you teach rules made up by humans.” What Really Makes People Unclean (Mark
text: Bimba ete beeyifo mɔ keyifo. Itsyise kawunsiǝ eto betidi eto asa ete baate lǝ mɔ ale eto kɛnyɛ.’ ” Asa Nnya Leetsyi Utu Kǝmiǝǝ Nnya Laanansǝ Utidi Nɛ.

ref: Stay away from those Pharisees! They are like blind people leading other blind people, and all of them will fall into a ditch.”
text: Nyaso biankpe mǝ kɛnyɛ. Bete fe bǝnǝmba ba nyɛnko bǝnǝmba bamba. Nya lǝ unǝmba ɔni yɛnko unǝmba bambaa, mǝ inuǝ ba tã ebudu banɔ bedufǝ lǝ kubiǝ kafo.”

ref: Jesus did not say a word. But the woman kept following along and shouting, so his disciples came up and asked him to send her away.
text: Fɛɛ Yesu ménfe kɛnyɛ ɔtɛyi wǝ kulesa. Mfó nya wǝ bakasebi lɛnyi besu balɛ wǝ lekpakpa bátɛyi wǝ bǝnkǝ, “La wǝ lǝ udu, itsyise utǝkǝko bo ɔlɛ bofã ketisǝ bo ato!”

ref: The food you put into your mouth doesn't make you unclean and unfit to worship God. The bad words that come out of your mouth are what make you unclean.”
text: Inni lesa nǝ leedufǝ utidi eto kɛnyɛ laabiasǝ wǝ, bomu etiki nnya laabɔ ketsyi lǝ wǝ kɛnyɛɛ, nnya laabiasǝ wǝ nɛ.”

ref: Then his disciples came over to him and asked, “Do you know you insulted the Pharisees by what you said?”
text: Nya wǝ bakasebi díbǝ wǝ ɔflɔ bátɛyi wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Eyi fǝnkǝ se Farisifɔ lɛnɔ asa nnya fátɛyii, éla mǝ kɛnyɛ lele tinti?”

ref: Peter replied, “What did you mean when you talked about the things that make people unclean?”
text: Petro átɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Lǝkǝ lekpa nǝmfo kasɔ ate bo.”

ref: Jesus then said: Don't any of you know by now what I am talking about?
text: Nya Yesu lɛtɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Nyalɛ betidi ba laannɔ asa kasɔ ete binsi binii?

ref: Don't you know that the food you put into your mouth goes into your stomach and then out of your body?
text: Binyi biǝ lesa saa nǝ didufǝ utidi lǝ kɛnyɛ, ayɛ kedufǝ wǝ lǝ kafo, nya weesie ni kabɔsǝ fe ɔflɔ kewi?

ref: But the words that come out of your mouth come from your heart. And they are what make you unfit to worship God.
text: Fɛɛ etiki nnya laabɔ ketsyi lǝ wǝ kɛnyɛ, aabɔ ketsyi lǝ wǝ utu kǝmiǝ. Nya nyamfo lǝǝtǝ utidi laanansǝ ǝsuǝ lǝ kesumu eto kusu ǝsuǝ nɛ.

ref: Out of your heart come evil thoughts, murder, unfaithfulness in marriage, vulgar deeds, stealing, telling lies, and insulting others.
text: Itsyise itsyi lǝ wǝ utu kǝmiǝ nya diǝtǝ weeyifo asa bua fe ditidilo, sɛsɔnɔdi nye disenkpo, setsyetɔ, kuyu ku bidiatɛyitɛyi ku betidi eto ayebiasǝbiasǝǝ, laabɔ ketsyi nɛ.

ref: These are what make you unclean. Eating without washing your hands will not make you unfit to worship God. A Woman's Faith (Mark
text: Asa nyamfo eto kakle laanansǝ utidi nɛ. Fɛɛ lǝ utidi mǝmflǝ kɔni fɛ udi lesa fe kase Farisifɔ laatee, nyamfo aannansǝ utidi.” Yesu Átsya Usio Utsyuǝ Eto Ubi.

ref: Jesus said, “I was sent only to the people of Israel! They are like a flock of lost sheep.”
text: Nya Yesu dítǝ mǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Israel bebi nte fe basamu ba lénya ɔflɔ hã Onanto lékpe mɛ nkǝ nsu.”

ref: Large crowds came and brought many people who were paralyzed or blind or lame or unable to talk. They placed them, and many others, in front of Jesus, and he healed them all.
text: Betidi kpǝ ǝbǝ wǝ ɔflɔ nya bákpa mba léba bankpoo kasɔ, bǝnǝmba, abafa, bamumu, ku befimfi bamba kpǝ báboko wǝ ɔflɔ, nya ótsya mǝ bufi.

ref: Jesus left and went to the territory near the towns of Tyre and Sidon.
text: Yesu édu mfó úsu lekpake nǝ lɛfi kǝmǝnkplɛ inuǝ ka ni Tiro ku Sidon ɔflɔ.

ref: Suddenly a Canaanite woman from there came out shouting, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on me! My daughter is full of demons.”
text: Mfó nya Kana eto usio utsyuǝ díbǝ Yesu ɔflɔ, nya ókafã bembembe kalɛ wǝ lekpakpa nkǝ, “Mɔ Onamute, Oka David eto kafo eto ubi, nyǝ mɛ kaminsǝ! Lɛwɔnɔ bua ǝbǝ disi lǝ mɔ ubisiobi ǝsuǝ nya dítǝ ɔnlɛ kanyi bunyǝ fefe!”

ref: The woman came closer. Then she knelt down and begged, “Please help me, Lord!”
text: Mfó nya usio ǝmfo díbǝ óse akonki lǝ wǝ ɔflɔ ɔ́tɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Mɔ Onamute, wunsǝko mɛ!”

ref: Jesus replied, “It isn't right to take food away from children and feed it to dogs.”
text: Yesu ǝtǝ wǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Yanlɛkɛ bǝ befi bekpefi eto didisa bato bakpe mǝ bakpa.”

ref: “Lord, this is true,” the woman said, “but even puppies get the crumbs that fall from their owner's table.”
text: Usio ǝmǝ ǝtǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Mɔ Onamute, ite nko sitinti. Fɛɛ bakpa bamǝ tsya aafo didisa wɛlɛwɛlɛ nǝ lélo lékpo kasɔ lǝ mǝ onamute eto ɔpunu kalɔ budii.”

ref: Jesus answered, “Dear woman, you really do have a lot of faith, and you will be given what you want.” At that moment her daughter was healed. Jesus Heals Many People
text: Mfó nya Yesu dítǝ wǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Eyifo usio wǝ nkpe bofokanɔ tinti! Lesa nǝ fanlɛ bɔbɛbɛɛ, lǝ ibǝ itǝ fǝ.” Nyaso lǝ lefe nǝmǝ tutuutu wǝ ubisiobi ǝmǝ ǝmuǝ ale. Yesu Átsya Betidi Kpǝ Bufi.

ref: From there, Jesus went along Lake Galilee. Then he climbed a hill and sat down.
text: Yesu édu mfó, nya ɔyɛ lǝ Galilea eto ditumbukplɛ ɔflɔ óbe kɔtini kutsyuǝ nya úsiǝ kasɔ.

ref: Everyone was amazed at what they saw and heard. People who had never spoken could now speak. The lame were healed, the paralyzed could walk, and the blind were able to see. Everyone was praising the God of Israel. Jesus Feeds Four Thousand (Mark
text: Iyifo betidi bamǝ yanii se bǝnyǝ bamumu nlɛ etiki budi, mba léba bankpoo kasɔ nlɛ botaka kayɛ, abafa nlɛ bɔyɛ, bǝnǝmba nlɛ asa bunyǝ nya bátɛsǝ Israel eto Onanto. Yesu Ǝtǝ Betidi Akpi Ana Lesa Bédi.

ref: Jesus called his disciples together and told them, “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat. I don't want to send them away hungry. They might faint on their way home.”
text: Yesu ákpadi wǝ bakasebi óboko wǝ ɔflɔ nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Sitiditu siamfo eto asa lɛ mɛ kaminsǝ, itsyise bǝbǝ mɛ ɔflɔ diyi tsyǝfǝ sɔ̃ɔ̃ nɛ, nya kulesa ente mɔmɔ boodi. Manla miǝ lǝ yani mǝ lǝ bedu ku kafo fufu, lǝ ǝnǝmi lǝ isu enako mǝ lǝ kusu.”

ref: His disciples said, “This place is like a desert. Where can we find enough food to feed such a crowd?”
text: Wǝ bakasebi átɔ wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Sieti buunyǝ didisa butǝ mǝ lǝ mfimnti mfo bǝ lǝ betidi ba kpenkpe mfo lǝ bedi bemi?”

ref: Jesus asked them how much food they had. They replied, “Seven small loaves of bread and a few little fish.”
text: Yesu átɔ mǝ nkǝ, “Didisa lesia nte mi?” Nya bátɛyi wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Abolo akuanse ku akpɛ kɛkɛ asiani ete nte.”

ref: After Jesus had told the people to sit down,
text: Nyaso Yesu átɛyi sitiditu siamǝ nkǝ bǝsiǝ kasɔ.

ref: he took the seven loaves of bread and the fish and gave thanks. He then broke them and handed them to his disciples, who passed them around to the crowds.
text: Úfi abolo akuanse ku akpɛ nyamǝ, nya ɔ́sɔ Onanto ani, nya úbudi nnya nya úfi útǝ wǝ bakasebi bamǝ. Nya bésie nnya bǝtǝ betidi bamǝ.

ref: Everyone ate all they wanted, and the leftovers filled seven large baskets.
text: Mǝmblɛ édi lesa nǝmǝ bemi. Nya se básɛ nnya wɛlɛwɛlɛ nnya díbuu, íyi etsyitsya akuanse.

ref: After Jesus had sent the crowds away, he got into a boat and sailed across the lake. He came to shore near the town of Magadan.
text: Se Yesu dítsyǝ betidi bamǝ kusu olosǝǝ, údufǝ lǝ ɔklɔ nya údu úsu Magadan eto lekpake eti.

ref: Don't you know by now that I am not talking to you about bread? Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees!
text: Ntsyǝ ibǝ fɛ biannɔ kasɔ biǝ inni didisa ǝsuǝ míndi mi etiki mintsyiko? Bebe sinini lǝ Farisifɔ ku Sadukifɔ lǝ mǝ ǝyǝnkisǝsa ǝsuǝ!”

ref: When Jesus and his disciples were near the town of Caesarea Philippi, he asked them, “What do people say about the Son of Man?”
text: Yesu ésu lekpake nǝ lɛfi Kaisarea Filipi eto umǝ. Mfó nya ɔ́tɔ wǝ bakasebi nkǝ, “Owoe betidi laatɛyi bǝ mɔ, Utidi Eto Ubi, ni?”

ref: Finally, the disciples understood that Jesus wasn't talking about the yeast used to make bread, but about the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees. Who Is Jesus? (Mark
text: Nyamfo ǝsuǝ fɛ bakasebi bamǝ lɛnɔ kasɔ bǝ inni ǝyǝnkisǝsa ǝsuǝ ɔnlɛ mǝ kɔlɛ bɔlɛ nɛ. Bomu lǝ asa nnya Farisifɔ ku Sadukifɔ laate ǝsuǝ ɔnlɛ. Petro Ǝlǝkǝ Ɔ́tɛyi Nkǝ Yesu Ni Utidi Wǝ Bákpo Ɔhile Bǝtsyǝ.

ref: He told them: If the sky is red in the evening, you say the weather will be good.
text: Fɛɛ Yesu ǝtǝ mǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Lǝ kofe lɛ diyo budufǝ nya binyǝ asɔmiloku lɛsɛ hǝǝ, biatɛyi biǝnkǝ kalesɛ ebusiǝ kusɛɛ.

ref: But if the sky is red and gloomy in the morning, you say it is going to rain. You can tell what the weather will be like by looking at the sky. But you don't understand what is happening now.
text: Nya kolesiǝntu lǝ kofe lɛ boto nya kawunsiǝ ditsyiko bona tũũ, biatɛyi biǝnkǝ ɔsɔmi ǝbǝ bɔni. Bialɛ ola akafo bote kase kawunsiǝ luubǝ kǝtsyǝ lǝ binyǝ asɔmiloku eto ditsyǝbi. Fɛɛ beso biǝmbofo asa nnya luubǝ lǝ lefe nǝmǝ bote!

ref: The disciples answered, “Some people say you are John the Baptist or maybe Elijah or Jeremiah or some other prophet.”
text: Bǝtǝ mbuayɛ bǝnkǝ, “Bǝtsyuǝ bǝ fǝ ni Ntuflǝsǝtidi Yohanes. Bǝtsyuǝ tsya bǝ fǝ ni Elia, nya babamba tsya bǝ Yeremiya nye Onanto eto Unyɛlǝkǝtidi bamba utsyuǝ feni.”

ref: From then on, Jesus began telling his disciples what would happen to him. He said, “I must go to Jerusalem. There the nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses will make me suffer terribly. I will be killed, but three days later I will rise to life.”
text: Idu lǝ lefe nǝmfó ɛntɔɔ, Yesu étsyiko wǝ bakasebi etiki bɔtɛyi kakpasǝ wuee nkǝ, “Ele bǝ nsu Yerusalem lǝ nsu nyǝ kanyi lǝ beninǝ, besumunyǝ ninǝ ku lǝ afi eto bateasa eto ani. Boolo mɛ. Fɛɛ lǝ diyi tsyǝfǝ ǝsuǝǝ, Onanto ebotakasǝ mɛ utsyiko ɔkɔmǝ nsinkli mbǝ nkpǝ.”

ref: Jesus then warned them, “Watch out! Guard against the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”
text: Yesu átɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Bebe sinini lǝ Farisifɔ ku Sadukifɔ eto ǝyǝnkisǝsa nnya baakpe lǝ asa ǝsuǝ.”

ref: The disciples talked this over and said to each other, “He must be saying this because we didn't bring along any bread.”
text: Mfó nya bétsyiko mǝ ǝsuǝ bɔtɔ bǝnkǝ, “Nnya bóentsyi didisa saa eso ɔnlɛ bɔtɛyi mmǝǝ.”

ref: The Son of Man will soon come in the glory of his Father and with his angels to reward all people for what they have done.
text: Itsyise Utidi Eto Ubi ebubǝ ku Onanto eto batɔnkpe lǝ Onanto eto diyǝnde ǝsuǝ, nya moofo utsyuǝ saa kayo lǝ keyifo ka úyifo ǝsuǝ.

ref: So I will call you Peter, which means “a rock.” On this rock I will build my church, and death itself will not have any power over it.
text: Nyaso nlɛ fǝ bɔtɛyi minkǝ fǝ Petro, fǝ diye ni leba. Nya leba nǝmfo ǝsuǝ mooto mɔ bafokanɔfɔ eto ditsyukpa nǝmǝ ńyǝnsǝ, nya ukpǝ eto ǝsuǝale kulaa tsya embutǝkǝ ni kato.

ref: Then Jesus asked, “But who do you say I am?”
text: Mfó nya Yesu lɛtɔ mǝ nkǝ, “Nya mi nkpǝ? Owoe biatɛyi biǝ wǝ mini?”

text: Simon Petro ǝtǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Fǝ ni Nwǝ Onanto Lékpo Ɔhile Utsyǝ. Fǝ ni Onanto wǝ nsi nkpǝ eto Ubi.”

ref: Jesus told him: Simon, son of Jonah, you are blessed! You didn't discover this on your own. It was shown to you by my Father in heaven.
text: Yesu ǝtǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Simon, Yona eto ubi, átɛyi nnya ɛlɛkɛ. Itsyise ɔnukualɛ ǝmfo mámbɔ utsyi lǝ utidinini saa eto kɛnyɛ. Bomu mɔ Anto wǝ nkpe katoo, wǝ dílǝkǝ nnya óte fǝ nɛ.

text: Muufi Onanto eto sekakedikɔ eto kasafui ntǝ fǝ. Lesa saa nǝ fookle lǝ kasɔ mfoo, bookle ni kato. Nya lesa saa nǝ foonyankli lǝ kasɔ mfoo, boonyankli ni tsya kato.”

text: Mfó nya Yesu lékpe wǝ bakasebi kɔfi nkǝ, “Biantɛyi kuutsyǝ saa biǝnkǝ mɔ ni Nwǝ Bákpo Ɔhile Bǝtsyǝ ǝmǝ.” Yesu Édi Etiki Itsyi Lǝ Wǝ Kanyinyǝnyǝ Ku Wǝ Ukpǝ Ǝsuǝ.

ref: Peter took Jesus aside and told him to stop talking like that. He said, “God would never let this happen to you, Lord!”

ref: Jesus turned to Peter and said, “Satan, get away from me! You're in my way because you think like everyone else and not like God.”
text: Yesu átsyi ɔ́wuninsǝ ɔ́tɛyi Petro nkǝ, “Obonsam, du mɛ lǝ ɔflɔ! Eyifo nwǝ eto eyifosa dińyǝ mɛ lǝ ǝsuǝ, itsyise fǝ nsusu bomfo menyifo Onanto eto nle, bomu utidinini eto nle.”

text: Nya Yesu lɛtɛyi wǝ bakasebi nkǝ, “Lǝ utsyuǝ aala nkǝ wǝǝtǝkǝko mɛɛ, ele bǝ ɔni wǝ ǝsuǝ lǝ utsyi wǝ uyikabie utǝkǝko mɛ.

text: Utsyuǝ saa wǝ laala nkǝ waata wǝ nkpǝ eto disii, woonyansǝ mu. Nya utsyuǝ saa wǝ loonyansǝ wǝ nkpǝ lǝ mɔ butǝkǝko ǝsuǝǝ, wuunyǝ mu.

ref: What will you gain, if you own the whole world but destroy yourself? What would you give to get back your soul?

ref: Jesus and his disciples returned to the crowd. A man knelt in front of him
text: Lefe nǝ Yesu ku wǝ bakasebi díbǝ báyo sitiditu siamǝ ɔflɔ, osani utsyuǝ ǝbǝ óse akonki lǝ Yesu ɔflɔ.

text: Nya ɔ́tɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Bonamute, nyǝ mɔ ubisaibi ǝmfo kaminsǝ! Ɔlɛ budukanɔ eto bufi bufi nya ɔnlɛ kanyi bunyǝ tinti. Lefe kpǝ weedu kanɔ kedufǝ lǝ ute nye ntu ǝsuǝ tsya.

ref: Then Jesus spoke sternly to the demon. It went out of the boy, and right then he was healed.

text: Lefe nǝ bakasebi bamǝ kenke díbǝ bátsya lǝ Galilea, Yesu átɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Boofi Utidi Eto Ubi bakpe betidi lǝ ani,

ref: who will kill him. But three days later he will rise to life.” All of this made the disciples very sad. Paying the Temple Tax
text: nya boolo wǝ. Fɛɛ lǝ diyi tsyǝfǝ ǝsuǝǝ, Onanto ebotakasǝ wǝ utsyiko ɔkɔmǝ oboko nkpǝ.” Nyamfo álo bakasebi bamǝ ani. Báfo Oko Lǝ Onanto Eto Disumuyo.

text: Lefe nǝ Yesu ku wǝ bakasebi díbǝ bédufǝ Kapernaum, Yudafɔ eto mba laafo Disumuyo eto oko ǝbǝ bátɔ Petro bǝnkǝ, “Mi oteasa tsya aafó Disumuyo eto oko ǝmǝ?”

ref: “Yes, he does,” Peter answered. After they had returned home, Jesus went up to Peter and asked him, “Simon, what do you think? Do the kings of this earth collect taxes and fees from their own people or from foreigners?”
text: Petro ǝtǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Ee, woafó.” Nya se Petro dísu údufǝ diyo, Yesu áka wǝ bɔtɔ nkǝ, “Simon, be ni fǝ nsusu? Bawoe eto ani ele bǝ bafo oko bǝtǝ kawunsiǝ eto baka? Bǝmǝnyǝ eto ani lee bafɔ?”

ref: Peter answered, “From foreigners.” Jesus replied, “Then their own people don't have to pay.
text: Petro ǝtǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Bafɔ.” Yesu ǝtǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Nyalɛ ete bǝ yanle bǝ bǝmǝnyǝ fo oko saa nɛ.

ref: But we don't want to cause trouble. So go cast a line into the lake and pull out the first fish you hook. Open its mouth, and you will find a coin. Use it to pay your taxes and mine.”
text: Fɛɛ se boanla boǝ boata kuutsyuǝ saa bubu esoo, su ditumbukplɛ eto kɛnyɛ lǝ ata osakɛ. Lɛkpɛ tutɛ nǝ fuulǝkǝǝ, ebunyǝ atabi lǝ ni kɛnyɛ nnya looyo afo boofǝ eto oko ǝmǝ. Lǝkǝ nya, lǝ esu afo bokufǝ eto oko.”

ref: If your eye causes you to sin, poke it out and get rid of it. You would be better off to go into life with only one eye than to have two eyes and be thrown into the fires of hell. The Lost Sheep (Luke
text: Nya lǝ fǝ dinǝmi ǝǝtǝ feeyifo lebua, lǝkǝ ni ato akpe! Ɛlɛkɛ itǝ fǝ bǝ nyǝ nkpǝ ku dinǝmi lɛwɛ enso bǝ kɛ ǝnǝmi ǝnuǝ lǝ befi fǝ bato bakpe lǝ ditefɛnku manlo kǝmiǝ. Yesu Áte Asa Itsyi Lǝ Osamu Wǝ Lenya Ǝsuǝ.

ref: But if you are as humble as this child, you are the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
text: Utidi wǝ ni ɔkplɛnyǝ kenke lǝ Onanto eto sekakedikɔɔ, wǝ ni utidi wǝ laabokosǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ kasɔ fe dikpefi nǝmfo eto okle.

ref: And when you welcome one of these children because of me, you welcome me. Temptations To Sin (Mark
text: “Nya utsyuǝ saa wǝ loofo bekpefi bamfo eto utsyuǝ lǝ mɔ disii, mɔɔ ini ófo. Mǝntǝ Lǝ Kuutsyuǝ Lǝ Udufǝ Lǝ Lebua Ǝsuǝ.

ref: If the follower refuses to listen to them, report the matter to the church. Anyone who refuses to listen to the church must be treated like an unbeliever or a tax collector. Allowing and Not Allowing
text: Nya lǝ mfó tsya ɔni woannɔ kasɔɔ, nyalɛ tǝ lǝ bafokanɔfɔ eto ditsyukpa nǝmǝ lǝ banɔ. Nya lǝ mfó tsya woannɔ kasɔɔ, lǝkǝ ato lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ fe utidi wǝ lǝmbǝ bofokanɔ nye nwǝ laafo umǝnko.

ref: It will be terrible for people who cause even one of my little followers to sin. Those people would be better off thrown into the deepest part of the ocean with a heavy stone tied around their necks!
text: “Fɛɛ lǝ utsyuǝ ebutǝ bekpefi ba léfo mɛ bánɔ mfo eto ɔni kplo lǝ onya kusu lǝ mɔ wǝ bofokanɔ ǝsuǝǝ, yɔɔlɛkɛ itǝ wǝ eso lǝ befi ukue batsyasǝ lǝ kukuǝ kplɛ bakpe wǝ lǝ simuǝ bato wǝ bakpe lǝ lekpo eto kaka letsyo, lǝ unǝ ntu ukpǝ.

ref: If your hand or foot causes you to sin, chop it off and throw it away! You would be better off to go into life paralyzed or lame than to have two hands or two feet and be thrown into the fire that never goes out.
text: Lǝ fǝ kɔni nye kokpa ǝǝtǝ feeyifo lebua, la ko ǝlǝkǝ ato akpe. Ɛlɛkɛ itǝ fǝ bǝ yɛ edufǝ lǝ nkpǝ kǝmiǝ kɔni kɔnwɛ nye kokpa kɔnwɛ enso bǝ yɛ ani ǝnuǝ nye akpa ǝnuǝ lǝ befi fǝ bato bakpe lǝ ditefɛnku manlo kǝmiǝ.

ref: If one of my followers sins against you, go and point out what was wrong. But do it in private, just between the two of you. If that person listens, you have won back a follower.
text: “Lǝ fǝ ɔyimitsyuǝ áta fǝ úbuu, su wǝ ɔflɔ, lǝ ǝlǝkǝ wǝ botakebu ate wǝ. Nya tǝ lǝ nyamfo lǝ ɛkɛ lǝ fonko wǝ eto nti ete. Lǝ ɔ́nɔ fǝ kasɔɔ, te fǝ esinkli eyifo lɔni ku fǝ ɔyimi nɛ.

ref: Don't be cruel to any of these little ones! I promise you their angels are always with my Father in heaven.
text: Itsyise Utidi Eto Ubi ǝbǝ nkǝ lǝ ofo betidi ba lénya kusu lǝ nkpǝ.

ref: I am sure that finding it would make you happier than having the
text: Nya lǝ únyǝ wǝǝ, mɔtɛyi mi miǝ wuunyǝ disuǝyuǝ tinti lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ enso awosi ɔwɔnɔmbǝ ɔwɔnɔmbǝ ba dímanya mfó ǝsuǝ.

ref: That's how it is with your Father in heaven. He doesn't want any of these little ones to be lost. When Someone Sins (Luke
text: Nko ke inte itǝ mi Anto wǝ nkpe kato nɛ, nkǝ woannla nkǝ bekpefi kɛkɛ bamfo eto kuutsyuǝ lǝ onya. Yɛnko Fǝ Ɔyimitsyuǝ Wǝ Léta Fǝ Úbu Lǝ Kusu Ǝsuǝ.

ref: But if that one refuses to listen, take along one or two others. The Scriptures teach that every complaint must be proven true by two or more witnesses.
text: Fɛɛ lǝ wǝmbɔnɔ fǝ kasɔɔ, tǝ lǝ betidi inuǝ nye itsyǝ lǝ banɔ ko mi lǝ adansiɛ seka eso, fe kase Awɔnisa Kɛnkɛ eto Kukue lɛtɛyi.

ref: I promise you God in heaven will allow whatever you allow on earth, but God will not allow anything you don't allow.
text: “Nyaso nlɛ mimblɛ bɔtɛyi minkǝ lesa saa nǝ biokle lǝ kasɔ mfoo, bookle ni kato. Nya lesa saa ke bionyankli lǝ kasɔ mfoo, boonyakli ni kato tsya.

ref: I promise that when any two of you on earth agree about something you are praying for, my Father in heaven will do it for you.
text: “Nya nsi nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi tsya minkǝ, lǝ mi inuǝ eto kɛnyɛ eyifo kani lǝ kasɔ mfo bɛnlɛ lesaatsyuǝ bɔtɔɔ, mɔ Anto wǝ nkpe kato ebuyifo ni utǝ mi.

ref: Whenever two or three of you come together in my name, I am there with you. An Official Who Refused To Forgive
text: Itsyise kaka mi inuǝ nye itsyǝ letsya lǝ mɔ diye ǝsuǝǝ, nkpe mi lenti.” Ele Bǝ Bukefi Bo Bayimitsyuǝ Eto Botakebu Bukatsyǝ Mǝ Lefe Saa.

ref: Peter came up to the Lord and asked, “How many times should I forgive someone who does something wrong to me? Is seven times enough?”
text: Nya Petro lɛnyi ɔ́fi Yesu nya ɔ́tɔ wǝ nkǝ, “Bonamute, lǝ mɔ ɔyimitsyuǝ ata mɛ ubuu, sesia ele bǝ mfi ntsyɛ wǝ? Sekuanse lee?”

ref: As he was doing this, one official was brought in who owed him
text: Kase útsyǝ kasɔ koo, bákpa osani utsyuǝ wǝ nsi wǝ kayo kplɛ báboko wǝ ɔflɔ.

ref: But he didn't have any money to pay what he owed. The king ordered him to be sold, along with his wife and children and all he owned, in order to pay the debt.
text: Kulesa ente wǝ wuufi ofo kayo kamǝ esoo, oka ǝmǝ ǝtǝ nkǝ bate wǝ ku wuusiǝ ku wǝ bebi ku lesa saa nǝ nte wǝ lǝ befi bafo kayo kamǝ.

ref: The official got down on his knees and began begging, “Have pity on me, and I will pay you every cent I owe!”
text: “Itǝ uyifoyifo ǝmfo áse akonki lǝ oka ǝmǝ ǝnǝmi ɔ́lɛ wǝ lekpakpa ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, ‘Tsyǝ ǝsuǝ ko mɛ, moofo fǝ kayo kamǝ kenke.’

ref: The king felt sorry for him and let him go free. He even told the official that he did not have to pay back the money.
text: Wǝ asa ǝnǝ oka ǝmǝ kafo, nyaso úfi kayo kamǝ kenke ɔ́tsyɛ wǝ nya óyani wǝ údu.

ref: The man got down on his knees and began begging, “Have pity on me, and I will pay you back.”
text: “Wǝ uyifotsyuǝ ǝmǝ álɛ wǝ lekpakpa ɔ́tɛyi wǝ nkǝ, ‘Tsyǝ ǝsuǝ ko mɛ, moofo fɛ kayo ka kenke minsi fǝ!’

ref: But the first official refused to have pity. Instead, he went and had the other official put in jail until he could pay what he owed.
text: “Fɛɛ ɔ́ni nya útǝ nkǝ beti wǝ bakpe lǝ Aba eto diyo isu ese lefe nǝ woofo kayo ka kenke unsi wǝ.

ref: When some other officials found out what had happened, they felt sorry for the man who had been put in jail. Then they told the king what had happened.
text: Se wǝ beyifotsyuǝ dínyǝ lesa nǝ díbǝǝ, ílǝ mǝ tinti nya bésu bátɛyi Oka ǝmǝ lesa saa nǝ díbǝ.

ref: The king called the first official back in and said, “You're an evil man! When you begged for mercy, I said you did not have to pay back a cent.
text: “Nyaso ókpadi wǝ uyifoyifo ǝmǝ óboko wǝ ɔflɔ nya ɔ́tɛyi wǝ nkǝ, ‘Fǝ ndomunyǝ bua! Mfi fǝ kayo ka fensi mɛ kenke ntsyɛ fǝ itsyise álɛ mǝ lekpakpa eso.

ref: The king was so angry that he ordered the official to be tortured until he could pay back everything he owed.
text: Kafo ányi Oka ǝmǝ tinti nya útǝ nkǝ bǝnǝ wǝ koto bakpe wǝ lǝ diyo isu ese lefe nǝ woofo kayo kamǝ kenke olosǝ.”

ref: That is how my Father in heaven will treat you, if you don't forgive each of my followers with all your heart.
text: Mfó Yesu lélosǝ nkǝ, “Nko ke mɔ Anto wǝ nkpe kato luuyifo utsyuǝ saa lǝ mi nti wǝ lembufi ɔtsyɛ wǝ ɔyimitsyuǝ itsyi taa wǝ lɛkɔɛsi ke nɛ.”

ref: When Jesus finished teaching, he left Galilee and went to the part of Judea east of the Jordan River.
text: Se Yesu lɛtɛyi asa nyamfo ólosǝǝ, údu lǝ Galilea úsu Yudea eto lɛkpɔ nǝ nkpe lǝ Yordan eto ketu eto lekpake nǝ nse.

ref: Some people are unable to marry because of birth defects or because of what someone has done to their bodies. Others stay single in order to serve God better. Anyone who can accept this teaching should do so.” Jesus Blesses Little Children (Mark
text: Esu kpǝ kpe nyaso betidi leemfi bǝsiǝ. Bǝtsyuǝ nko bále mǝ nɛ. Bǝtsyuǝǝ, betidi díyifo mǝ nko. Bǝtsyuǝ tsya emfi bǝsiǝ itsyise bala bǝ beefi mǝ ǝsuǝ bǝtǝ Onanto eto sekakedikɔ eto keyifo. Nwǝ loofo wǝ ǝsuǝ bufi kǝtǝ asatete nyamfoo, tǝ lǝ uyifo nko.” Bákpa Bekpefi Baboko Yesu Ɔflɔ.

ref: Large crowds followed him, and he healed their sick.
text: Betidi kpǝ ǝtǝkǝko wǝ nya ótsya mǝ efimbi, efimbi mfó.

ref: Some Pharisees wanted to test Jesus. They came up to him and asked, “Is it right for a man to divorce his wife for just any reason?”
text: Mfó nya Farisifɔ bǝtsyuǝ díbǝ wǝ ɔflɔ bǝ baato wǝ babe, nya bátɔ wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Bo kɔfi ǝtǝ kusu nkǝ osani ni wǝ usiǝ lǝ kusu saa ola ǝsuǝ?”

ref: Jesus answered, “Don't you know in the beginning the Creator made a man and a woman?
text: Yesu ǝtǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Biankla tɔ lǝ Awɔnisa Kɛnkɛ bǝ taa ketsyikokɔɔ, nwǝ díyifo kawunsiǝǝ, usio ku osani úyifo betidi?

ref: That's why a man leaves his father and mother and gets married. He becomes like one person with his wife.
text: Nya Onanto lɛtɛyi nkǝ, ‘Lǝ nyamfo eso osani luudu lǝ wǝ anto ku ambe utsyǝ nya wuufi kusiǝ, nya mǝ inuǝ bamǝ loofiani utidi ɔni.’

ref: Then they are no longer two people, but one. And no one should separate a couple God has joined together.”
text: Itsyise lǝ nnya kamaa, bensiyifo betidi inuǝ. Bomu bafiani utidi ɔni. Nyaso yanle bǝ utidi sense lesa nǝ Onanto dífi ɔ́kɔ lǝ bǝtsyuǝ.”

ref: The Pharisees asked Jesus, “Why did Moses say a man could write out divorce papers and send his wife away?”
text: Farisifɔ bamǝ átɔ wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Nyalɛ beso Moses dítǝ kusu nkǝ ele bǝ osani tǝ wǝ usiǝ kukue nkǝ ɔlɛ wǝ bɔni fɛ lǝ ɔni wǝ?”

ref: Jesus replied, “You are so heartless! That's why Moses allowed you to divorce your wife. But from the beginning God did not intend it to be that way.
text: Yesu ǝtǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Mi letotidi eso Moses dítǝ kusu nkǝ bɛni bese ku bǝsiǝ nɛ. Yɛntsyǝ nko lǝ kasɔ ketsyikokɔ.

ref: I say if your wife has not committed some terrible sexual sin, you must not divorce her to marry someone else. If you do, you are unfaithful.”
text: Nyaso nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi minkǝ osani wǝ ke lɛni wǝ usiǝ, nya inni lǝ disenkpo eso nya úsu úfi usio bambaa, ókpo kusiǝ.”

ref: Jesus told them, “Only those people who have been given the gift of staying single can accept this teaching.
text: Yesu átɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Inni utsyuǝ saa loofo asatete nyamfo bofo nko, enso mba Onanto dítǝ nnya nko hã.

ref: Some people brought their children to Jesus, so he could place his hands on them and pray for them. His disciples told the people to stop bothering him.
text: Betidi bǝtsyuǝ ákpa bekpefi báboko Yesu ɔflɔ bǝ lǝ ufi ani utǝkǝ lǝ mǝ, lǝ oto ola utǝ mǝ, fɛɛ wǝ bakasebi ényenki bákpe betidi bamǝ.

ref: A man came to Jesus and asked, “Teacher, what good thing must I do to have eternal life?”
text: Osani utsyuǝ ǝbǝ Yesu ɔflɔ diyi lɔni nya ɔ́tɔ wǝ nkǝ, “Oteasa, nte lesa sɛɛ nǝ ele bǝ nyifo fɛ lǝ nnyǝ nkpǝ manlo?”

ref: “Which ones?” the man asked. Jesus answered, “Do not murder. Be faithful in marriage. Do not steal. Do not tell lies about others.
text: Ɔ́tɔ nkǝ, “Nte afi nnya?” Yesu átɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Manlo utidi, mendi sɛsɔnɔ, menyu, manla fuutsyuǝ kɛnyɛ,

ref: After Jesus had placed his hands on the children, he left. A Rich Young Man (Mark
text: Nya Yesu dífi wǝ ani útǝkǝ lǝ mǝ ókpo mǝ atsyɔ, nya údu lǝ mǝ ɔflɔ. Yɔɔkɛ Ale Itǝ Batabinyǝ Bǝ Bedufǝ Onanto Eto Sekakedikɔ.

ref: Jesus said to him, “Why do you ask me about what is good? Only God is good. If you want to have eternal life, you must obey his commandments.”
text: Mfó Yesu dítǝ wǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Beso fanlɛ mɛ kasɛɛ ka ele bǝ beyifo bɔtɔ? Utidi ɔni hã nkpe wǝ ni ɔsɛɛ. Lǝ ala fǝ ǝǝnyǝ nkpǝ manloo, yɛ lǝ Onanto eto afi nyamǝ ǝsuǝ.”

ref: But Jesus said, “Let the children come to me, and don't try to stop them! People who are like these children belong to God's kingdom.”
text: Mfó nya Yesu lɛtɛyi wǝ bakasebi bamǝ nkǝ, “Bitǝ bekpefi kusu lǝ bǝbǝ mɛ ɔflɔ, nya bienti mǝ kusu, itsyise Onanto eto sekakedikɔ eyifo fǝ mba laabokosǝ ǝsuǝ kasɔ fe ekpefi bamfo eto okle eto kale.”

ref: But many who are now first will be last, and many who are last will be first.
text: Fɛɛ betidi kpǝ ba ntɔ sɛtɔ mɔmɔɔ, ebubǝ bafe kama. Nya mba ntǝkǝ kama mɔmɔɔ, ebubǝ bafe batɔ sɛtɔ.

ref: Respect your father and mother. And love others as much as you love yourself.”
text: bu fǝ anto ku fǝ ambe, nya lǝ ala fǝ ɔyimitsyuǝ fe kase faala fǝ ǝsuǝ.”

ref: The young man said, “I have obeyed all of these. What else must I do?”
text: Mfó nya okosobi ǝmfo dítǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, “Mbuyifo lǝ afi nyamfo kenke ǝsuǝ. Be disi ibu bǝ nyifo?”

ref: Jesus replied, “If you want to be perfect, go sell everything you own! Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come and be my follower.”
text: Yesu átɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Lǝ aala fǝ aaflɔɔ, su lǝ esu efi fǝ lesa saa nǝ nte fǝ ate. Nya lǝ efi atabi nnya fǝnyǝ esu ǝtǝ bakombo, nya fuunyǝ bikǝ ǝtsyǝ kato nɛ. Nyamfo eto kamaa, sinkli ǝbǝ ǝtǝkǝko mɛ.”

ref: When the young man heard this, he went away sad, because he was very rich.
text: Fɛɛ se okosobi ǝmǝ lɛnɔ nyamfoo, údu ku bulǝ lǝ lɛkɔɛsi. Itsyise bikǝ kpǝ akɛ wǝ.

ref: Jesus said to his disciples, “I tell you, it's terribly hard for rich people to get into the kingdom of heaven!
text: Mfó nya Yesu lɛtɛyi wǝ bakasebi nkǝ, “Nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi kakpasǝ wuee minkǝ, yɔɔkɛ ale itǝ batabinyǝ bǝ bedufǝ Onanto eto sekakedikɔ.

ref: In fact, it's easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to get into God's kingdom.”
text: Nsi nlɛ bɔtɛyi bio minkǝ, iyuǝ itǝ ɔtɔmini ku lekoko bǝ ɔyɛ lǝ okabikǝ eto ofo ofe enso bǝ otabinyǝ dufǝ lǝ Onanto eto sekakedikɔ.”

ref: When the disciples heard this, they were greatly surprised and asked, “How can anyone ever be saved?”
text: Lefe nǝ bakasebi bamǝ lɛnɔ nyamfoo, íyifo mǝ yanii, nyaso bátɔ bǝnkǝ, “Nyalɛ owoe ko luunyǝ nkpǝ manlo?”

ref: Jesus looked straight at them and said, “There are some things people cannot do, but God can do anything.”
text: Yesu ábe mǝ tũũ nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Kawunsiǝ eto utidi saa ǝmbofo nyamfo buyifo. Fɛɛ Onanto, wǝ ebofo lesa saa buyifo.”

ref: Peter replied, “Remember, we have left everything to be your followers! What will we get?”
text: Mfó nya Petro lɛtɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Bee! Bóayani lesa saa bútsyǝ bǝ lǝ butǝkǝko fǝ. Be bo luunyǝ?”

ref: Jesus answered: Yes, all of you have become my followers. And so in the future world, when the Son of Man sits on his glorious throne, I promise you will sit on twelve thrones to judge the twelve tribes of Israel.
text: Yesu átɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi sitinti minkǝ, lefe nǝ mɔ, Utidi Eto Ubi, luubǝ lǝ mɔ diyǝnde ǝsuǝ mbǝ nsiǝ lǝ mɔ lekankpomɛ ǝsuǝ lǝ kawunsiǝ fɔfɔ kamǝǝ, mi lefosi inuǝ ba dítǝkǝko mɛ tsya ebusiǝ lǝ akankpomɛ ǝsuǝ bǝ lǝ bekponko Israel bebi eto lefosi mmfo nnuǝ bomǝ lekpo.

ref: All who have given up home or brothers and sisters or father and mother or children or land for me will be given
text: Nyaso utsyuǝ saa wǝ léyani diyo nye bayimisani nye bayimisio nye anto nye ambe nye bebi nye kakuɛ utsyǝ nkǝ lǝ utǝkǝko mɛ esoo, ebunyǝ nnya kayofofo kolofa ku kama. Nya Onanto ebutǝ wǝ nkpǝ bo lǝmbɔkɛ kalosǝkɔ.

ref: As Jesus was telling what the kingdom of heaven would be like, he said: Early one morning a man went out to hire some workers for his vineyard.
text: “Onanto eto sekakedikɔ ale eyibibi eto ɔkuɛmfo utsyuǝ wǝ létaka ku olesiǝtu nkǝ weesu ɔbɛbɛ diyi eto beyifoyifo bǝtsyuǝ lǝ beyifo keyifo bǝtǝ wǝ lǝ ɔkuɛ.

ref: After he had agreed to pay them the usual amount for a day's work, he sent them off to his vineyard.
text: Ɔkuɛmfo ǝmǝ ku beyifoyifo bamǝ átɔnɔ lǝ diyi eto keyifo eto kayofofo ǝsuǝ, ini sika flubia sɔni eto atabi, nya bédu bésu eyibibi eto ɔkuɛ ǝmǝ.

ref: About nine that morning, the man saw some other people standing in the market with nothing to do.
text: “Se ɛsɔ abombo ɔwɔnɔmbǝǝ, osani ǝmǝ esi úsu asi nya únyǝ se basani bǝtsyuǝ ńyǝ mfó banlɛ kulesa buyifo.

ref: He promised to pay them what was fair, if they would work in his vineyard.
text: Nyaso ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, ‘Mi tsya su biyifo keyifo lǝ mɔ eyibibi eto ɔkuɛ lǝ mfo mi kayo ka dińyǝ itǝ.’

ref: So they went. At noon and again about three in the afternoon he returned to the market. And each time he made the same agreement with others who were loafing around with nothing to do.
text: Nyaso bésu ɔkuɛ ǝmǝ. “Nya lǝ lefosi abombo ǝnuǝ ku lǝ kakɔmi eto abombo ǝtsyǝ eto lefee, usi úyifo nko ke.

ref: Finally, about five in the afternoon the man went back and found some others standing there. He asked them, “Why have you been standing here all day long doing nothing?”
text: Se ibu sɛkɛ lǝ abombo anɔ lǝ ɛsɔɔ, usi úsu asi mfó nya únyǝ betidi bǝtsyuǝ nsi ńyǝ mfó. Ɔ́tɔ mǝ nkǝ, ‘Beso binyǝ mfo diyi wɔlɔlɔlɔ bɛnlɛ diyi bobiasǝ keyifo menyifo menyifo?’

ref: “Because no one has hired us,” they answered. Then he told them to go work in his vineyard.
text: “Bǝtǝ wǝ mbuayɛ bǝnkǝ, ‘Kuutsyuǝ saa mǝntǝ bo keyifo nkǝ buyifo.’ “Nyaso ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, ‘Nyalɛ, mi tsya su mɔ eyibibi eto ɔkuɛ bisu biyifo keyifo.’

ref: That evening the owner of the vineyard told the man in charge of the workers to call them in and give them their money. He also told the man to begin with the ones who were hired last.
text: “Se ditsyitsyo lefe léyoo, ɔkuɛnsate ǝmǝ átɛyi beyifoyifo eto uninǝ nkǝ, ‘Kpadi beyifoyifo bamǝ lǝ afo mǝ kayo, tsyiko lǝ mba díbǝ fɛfɛ ǝsuǝ, lǝ esu alosǝ lǝ mba leka bubǝ ǝsuǝ.’

ref: When the workers arrived, the ones who had been hired at five in the afternoon were given a full day's pay.
text: “Báfo betidi ba díbǝ keyifo ku ditsyitsyo eto abombo anɔ eto utsyuǝ saa sika flubia sɔni sɔni.

ref: they began complaining to the owner of the vineyard.
text: Se báfo atabi nyamǝǝ, betsyiko bunyenki kakpe ɔkuɛnsate ǝmǝ bǝnkǝ,

ref: They said, “The ones who were hired last worked for only one hour. But you paid them the same that you did us. And we worked in the hot sun all day long!”
text: ‘Betidi fɛfɛ ba báfo éyifo keyifo lebombosɔsɔ lɔni hã, nya bomba mfo díńyǝ lǝ kofe buyifo keyifo diyi wɔlɔlɔlɔ tsyaa, kayofofo kani ete bǝtǝ bo!’

ref: The owner answered one of them, “Friend, I didn't cheat you. I paid you exactly what we agreed on.
text: “Mfó ɔkuɛnsate ǝmǝ lɛtɛyi mǝ ɔni nkǝ, ‘Ɔyimitsyuǝ, nɔ. Mántafa fǝ. Lee, inni diyi lɔni eto keyifo eto kayofofo ka ni sika flubia sɔni ǝsuǝ bókpe asi?

ref: Take your money now and go! What business is it of yours if I want to pay them the same that I paid you?
text: Nyaso fo fǝ kayofofo lǝ edu esu diyo. Iyifo mɔ lelabi miǝ mǝǝtǝ utidi fɛfɛ ǝmfo kayofofo fe kase mínkǝtǝ fǝ ke.

ref: Don't I have the right to do what I want with my own money? Why should you be jealous, if I want to be generous?”
text: Lee, mimbǝ kusu meeyifo ku mɔ atabi kase maala? Lee, mɔ kafoɛkpɛ ka meeyifo nlɛ fǝ ǝnǝmi bɔsɛ?’ ”

ref: Jesus then said, “So it is. Everyone who is now last will be first, and everyone who is first will be last.” Jesus Again Tells about His Death (Mark
text: Nyaso Yesu álosǝ ɔtɛyi nkǝ, “Betidi ba ni bafɛfɛ ebofiani betutɛ nya mba ni betutɛ loofiani bafɛfɛ.” Yesu Esi Údi Etiki Ditsyǝfǝ Itsyi Lǝ Wǝ Ukpǝ Ǝsuǝ.

ref: When Jesus heard them, he stopped and asked, “What do you want me to do for you?”
text: Yesu ǝkǝnǝ únyǝ nya ókpadi mǝ nkǝ bǝbǝ nya ɔ́tɔ mǝ nkǝ, “Be bela biǝ nyimfo ntǝ mi?”

ref: Jesus felt sorry for them and touched their eyes. At once they could see, and they became his followers.
text: Mǝ asa ǝnǝ Yesu kafo nya utidi mǝ ǝnǝmi. Lamfolamfo, mǝ ǝnǝmi ásini nya bǝnyǝ asa, nya bǝtǝkǝko wǝ.

ref: The crowd told them to be quiet, but they shouted even louder, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on us!”

text: Nko ke ini nɛ bǝ mɔ, Utidi Eto Ubi, mǝmbǝ miǝ besumu mɛ. Bomu bǝ lǝ nsumu nya lǝ mfi mɔ nkpǝ ntǝ lǝ betidi kpǝ eto disi.” Yesu Átsya Bǝnǝmba Inuǝ.

text: Nya lesa saa nǝ biɔtɔ Onanto lǝ olatoto ǝsuǝ lǝ bofokanɔ ǝsuǝǝ, biunyǝ ni.” Bátɔ Yesu Ǝsuǝale Nnya Onkpe.

text: Owoe ɔflɔ Yohanes léfo ǝsuǝale fɛ úflǝsǝ betidi ntuu? Onanto ɔflɔ lee, nye lǝ utidi ɔflɔ?” Mfó bétsyiko mǝ ǝsuǝ bɔtɔ kanɔ bǝ, “Be ele bǝ bɔtɛyi? Lǝ bɔtɛyi boǝ Onanto ɔflɔɔ, wɔɔtɔ bo nkǝ, ‘Nyalɛ beso biamfo Yohanes bɛnɔ?’

text: Itsyise Ntuflǝsǝtidi Yohanes ǝbǝ mi ɔflɔ nkǝ wǝǝbǝ ote mi kusu sɛɛ ko biɔyɛ, fɛɛ bíamfo wǝ bɛnɔ. Fɛɛ beyu ku basɔnɔdi bomu áfo wǝ bánɔ. Nya se bínyǝ nyamfo bítsyǝ lekpo tsyaa, bíamfloma mi nsusu biǝ biafo wǝ bɛnɔ.

text: Se lefe léyo bǝ balɛ eyibibi nyamǝǝ, ɔkuɛnsate ǝmfo ákpe wǝ ndomufɔ nkǝ besu bafo wǝ kedikɔ baboko wǝ.

text: Nyamfo eto kamaa, osate ǝmǝ esi ótsyese ndomufɔ bamba ba nso mba kɔ utu. Nya beyifoyifo bamǝ disi béyifo mǝ tsya nko ke.

ref: So they grabbed him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
text: Nyaso bǝmuǝ wǝ nya báto wǝ bakpe lǝ ɔkpɔ ǝmǝ eto kama, nya bálo wǝ.”

text: Mfó nya Yesu lɛtɔ mǝ nkǝ, “Mɔmɔɔ, lǝ eyibibi eto ɔkuɛnsate ǝmǝ ǝbǝǝ, be wuuyifo beyifoyifo bamǝ?”

text: Bǝtǝ mbuayɛ bǝnkǝ, “Woolo betidi bua bamfó kaminsǝ mǝnyǝmǝnyǝ, ufi eyibibi eto ɔkuɛ ǝmǝ okpe beyifoyifo bamba ba luutǝ wǝ, wǝ kutsyǝ lǝ balɛ eyibibi nyamǝ.”

ref: Jesus replied, “You surely know that the Scriptures say, ‘The stone the builders tossed aside is now the most important stone of all. This is something the Lord has done, and it is amazing to us.’
text: Yesu átɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Bíankla tɔ lesa nǝ nkpe lǝ Awɔnisa Kɛnkɛ eto Kukue ǝsuǝ bǝnkǝ, ‘Leba nǝ mba laato ayo lɛni bǝ dimbǝ kɔnɔɔ, ni bomu díbǝ lefiani nǝ nso bofe lǝ afɛfɛ kenke nti. Bonamute ni nwǝ díyifo nyamfo. Sitintii, iyifo lesa nǝ laanɔ bobe tinti!’ ”

ref: I tell you God's kingdom will be taken from you and given to people who will do what he demands.

text: Nya utsyuǝ saa ke lɔɔtsyinini udu ɔnɔ lǝ leba nǝ báni mfó ǝsuǝǝ, wuufǝsǝ blublublu. Nya lǝ ibǝ bǝ leba nǝmǝ tsya asɔ utsyuǝ lǝ ǝsuǝǝ, dioba wǝ dikuǝ wǝ blublublu.”

ref: So they looked for a way to arrest Jesus. But they were afraid to, because the people thought he was a prophet.

text: Nya Yesu lélosǝ nkǝ, “Onanto ákpadi betidi kpǝ, fɛɛ esiani ete úlǝkǝ lǝ mǝ nti.” Yesu Édi Etiki Itsyi Lǝ Umǝnkofofo Ǝsuǝ.

text: Bátɛyi wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Oteasa, Moses átɛyi bo nkǝ, lǝ osani utsyuǝ ǝkpǝ nya óyani wǝ usiǝ utsyǝ nya wonko wǝ dímanle bebii, elebɛ osani ǝmǝ eto ɔyimisani fi ɔkɔsiǝ ǝmǝ ufi. Lǝ wonko wǝ ale bebii, boofi bebi bamǝ fe osani ǝmǝ díkpe eto bebi.

ref: Once again Jesus used stories to teach the people:
text: Yesu esi óté mǝ asa ɛyɛ lǝ akpá ǝsuǝ nkǝ,

ref: The kingdom of heaven is like what happened when a king gave a wedding banquet for his son.
text: “Onanto eto sekakedikɔ te fe mmǝ. Lefe lɔnii, Oka utsyuǝ édi kusiǝkpakpa eto diyisɛɛ útǝ wǝ ubisaibi.

text: Óla wǝ beyifoyifo ókpe nkǝ besu bakpo betidi ba ókla utsyǝ nkǝ bafe bǝbǝ diyisɛɛ nǝmǝ kedikɔ. Fɛɛ bánla bǝ bǝǝbǝ.

text: “Nyaso óla wǝ beyifoyifo bamba ókpe nkǝ batɛyi betidi ba ókla utsyǝ nkǝ, ‘Lesalidife nǝmǝ eboyo! Bálo mɔ banantsyui ku banatsyui eto akpɔ nnya nkpe ayinkua bufi bɔlɛ mi kama bobe. Nya búkalɔ lesa saa bútsyǝ. Nyaso bibǝ kusiǝ komǝ kakpakɔ!’

text: Biɔnɔ ama eto kudu ku ama eto etiki lǝ katsyontsyokɔ ku kaka lɛfi. Fɛɛ bíǝntǝ lǝ nyamfo lǝ ekpe mi sikpi. Ele bǝ asa nyamfo bǝ, fɛɛ yante bǝ kalosǝkɔ eboyo tɔ.

ref: Later, as Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, his disciples came to him in private and asked, “When will this happen? What will be the sign of your coming and of the end of the world?”
text: Se Yesu dísu unsi lǝ Nnuǝbi eto Eyi eto Kɔtini ǝsuǝǝ, wǝ bakasebi ǝbǝ wǝ ɔflɔ buɛɛ nya bátɔ wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Tɛyi bo lefe nǝ kawunsiǝ eto kalosǝkɔ luubǝ ku lesa nǝ luubǝ nnya loote bǝ fǝ businkli kǝbǝ eto lefe eboyo.”

ref: Nations and kingdoms will go to war against each other. People will starve to death, and in some places there will be earthquakes.
text: Nsɔ eto betidi ebɔkpɔ ama ku mǝ bǝtsyuǝ. Nya sekakedikɔ ebotaka sińyǝ lǝ se utsyuǝ ǝsuǝ. Nya kukpɛnsɛ luubǝ lǝ nlebe kpǝ. Nya nsɔ tsya luutinkǝ lǝ nlebe nlebe.

ref: It will be a terrible time for women who are expecting babies or nursing young children.
text: Fɛɛ mbɔbɔɛ mǝǝtǝ bafonlɛ ku bebiambe ba nlɛ bebisambi bɔyimisǝ lǝ lefe nǝmfo ǝsuǝ.

ref: Jesus answered: Don't let anyone fool you.
text: Yesu átɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Bebe kukɔnɔ biǝ kuutsyuǝ saa manwɛ mi okpe.

ref: But this is just the beginning of troubles.
text: Nyamfo kenke ebuyifo fe kase kalelefo lǝǝtsyǝ usio kasɔ ku bunǝ.

ref: You will be arrested, punished, and even killed. Because of me, you will be hated by people of all nations.
text: “Lǝ lefe nǝmfóo, boomuǝ mi befi mi bakpe lǝ ani bǝtǝ bǝ bate mi ohiã, nya boolo mi kulaa tsya. Kawunsiǝ eto betidi kenke ebukisi mi lǝ mɔ disi eso.

ref: Many will give up and will betray and hate each other.
text: Betidi kpǝ ebonyansǝ mǝ bofokanɔ. Boofi mǝ bǝtsyuǝ bakpe lǝ ani bǝtǝ balo nya bookisi mǝ bayimi bǝtsyuǝ.

ref: Many false prophets will come and fool a lot of people.
text: Betidi kpǝ ebɔbɔ batɛyi bidia bǝnkǝ mǝ ni Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi nya bɔɔwɛ betidi kpǝ bakpe.

ref: Someone may say, “Here is the Messiah!” or “There he is!” But don't believe it.
text: “Nyaso lǝ utsyuǝ átɛyi mi nkǝ, ‘Bebe! Nnyǝ Nwǝ Onanto Lékpo Ɔhile Utsyǝ nkpe mfo o-o,’ nye ‘Nnyǝ wǝ nkpe mmǝǝ,’ mamfo wǝ anɔ.

ref: And pray that you won't have to escape in winter or on a Sabbath.
text: Bɛlɛ Onanto lekpakpa biǝ mmi ditsyetsyife nǝmǝ mǝntǝkǝ lǝ lɛyɔ lefe nye Ɔnwɛditsyǝyi ǝsuǝ.

ref: Someday you will see that “Horrible Thing” in the holy place, just as the prophet Daniel said. Everyone who reads this must try to understand!
text: “Buinyǝ ekpinsa nyamǝ luubǝ ińyǝ lǝ kakɛnkɛkɔ kamǝ fe kase Onanto eto unyǝlǝkǝtidi Daniel lɛtɛyi útsyǝ. Yuuńyǝ lǝ kakɛkɛnkɔ kamǝ. Nya fǝ ke lékla kukue komfoo, nɔ nyamfo kasɔ wuee.

ref: If you are living in Judea at that time, run to the mountains.
text: “Nya lǝ lefe nǝmfóo, mimba nkpe lǝ Yuda eto kasɔ, tsyetsyi besu atini ǝsuǝ.

ref: If you are out in the field, don't go back for your coat.
text: Utidi wǝ nkpe lǝ ɔkuɛ mensi sinkli ubǝ oto nkǝ wǝǝbǝ ufi wǝ kafia.

ref: If God doesn't make the time shorter, no one will be left alive. But because of God's chosen ones, he will make the time shorter.
text: Fɛɛ Onanto ébose nnya uyi útsyǝ nkǝ wuulǝkǝ eyi nyamǝ ubu. Lenni nko esoo, eni utidi sa embubu usiǝ lǝ nkpǝ. Fɛɛ lǝ betidi ba Onanto díkǝlǝkǝ utsyǝ esoo, wuulǝkǝ eyi nyamǝ ubu.

ref: So when you see all these things happening, you will know the time has almost come.
text: Nko ke lǝ binyǝ asa nnya kenke mínkatɛyi míntɔnsǝǝ, bete biǝ kalosǝkɔ kamǝ eboyo kalo.

ref: When the Son of Man appears, things will be just as they were when Noah lived.
text: Utidi Eto Ubi eto bubǝ ebuyifo fe kase íbǝ lǝ Noa eto lefe.

ref: No one knows the day or hour. The angels in heaven don't know, and the Son himself doesn't know. Only the Father knows.
text: “Kuutsyuǝ saa enyi diyi nye lefe nǝmǝ kalosǝkɔ luubǝ. Onanto eto Batɔnkpe nye Ubi ǝmǝ kulaa tsya enyi. Anto hã nyi.

ref: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day the flood came and Noah went into the big boat.
text: Lǝ lefe nǝ lɛtɔ ntu eto buyi bomǝǝ, betidi ékedi, bǝkǝnǝ, basani ku besio ákakpa bǝtsyuǝ isu ese diyi fɛfɛ nǝ Noa dídufǝ lǝ kufue komǝ kafo.

ref: They didn't know anything was happening until the flood came and swept them all away. This is how it will be when the Son of Man appears.
text: Fɛɛ bánte lesa nǝ nlɛ bubǝ isu ese lefe nǝ ɔsɔmi lɛni, ntu díyi bókpa mǝmblɛ kenke búdunko. Nko ke yuutsyǝ lǝ Utidi Eto Ubi eto dibǝfe tsya nɛ.

ref: Two men will be in the same field, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
text: Lǝ lefe nǝmfóo, basani inuǝ ebuńyǝ lǝ ɔkuɛ bekeyifo keyifo fɛ wookpa ɔni udunko nya uubu ɔni bǝtsyǝ.

ref: Two women will be together grinding grain, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
text: Besio inuǝ ebuńyǝ bǝkǝkuǝ antabi, fɛɛ Onanto ebutsyi ɔni udunko nya uubu ɔni utsyǝ.

ref: So be on your guard! You don't know when your Lord will come.
text: “Nyaso bitǝkǝ ǝnǝ, itsyise binnyi diyi nǝ mi Onamute luubǝ.

ref: Homeowners never know when a thief is coming, and they are always on guard to keep one from breaking in.
text: Lǝ oyosate áte dinǝ uyu luubǝ wǝ keyukɔ, eni wuutǝkǝ ǝnǝ ukase ni. Nya weembutǝ kusu nkǝ uyu ǝmǝ dufǝ, oba wǝ diyo uyu wǝ.

ref: Always be ready! You don't know when the Son of Man will come. Faithful and Unfaithful Servants (Luke
text: Nko ke ele bǝ fintsya muǝ ǝsuǝ ǝtsyǝ, itsyise Utidi Eto Ubi ǝmǝ ebubǝ lǝ lefe nǝ fannlɛ wǝ kusu bobe. Ele Bǝ Uyifoyifo Yifo Asa Lǝ Kusu Ǝsuǝ.

ref: But suppose one of the servants thinks the master won't return until late.
text: Fɛɛ lǝ uyifoyifo bua unii, wɔɔtɛyi wǝ ǝsuǝ nkǝ, ‘Mɔ Onamute embubǝ mɔmɔ!’

ref: Who are faithful and wise servants? Who are the ones the master will put in charge of giving the other servants their food supplies at the proper time?
text: “Owoe ni uyifoyifo sɛɛ wǝ ni obesibe? Wǝ ni uyifoyifo wǝ, wǝ Onamute dífi útsyǝ lǝ wǝ beyifotsyuǝ eto kɛnyɛ, nkǝ úkǝtǝ mǝ lesa lǝ lefe ǝsuǝ.

ref: Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job.
text: Nte disuǝyuǝ nǝ eto okle yuudi itǝ uyifoyifo ǝmfo lǝ wǝ Onamute ǝbǝ unyǝ wǝ ɔnlɛ buyifo lǝ kusu ǝsuǝ!

ref: You may be sure a servant who is always faithful will be put in charge of everything the master owns.
text: Nlɛ fǝ bɔtɛyi sitinti minkǝ Onamute ǝmfo ebufi uyifoyifo ǝmfo utsyǝ lǝ wǝ bikǝ kenke eto kɛnyɛ.

ref: Suppose this evil servant starts beating the other servants and eats and drinks with people who are drunk.
text: Nyaso wuutsyiko wǝ beyifoyifotsyuǝ bɔblɛ, nya ukedi, ukǝnǝ ku betekpe.

ref: If that happens, the master will surely come on a day and at a time when the servant least expects him.
text: Nya wǝ Onamute ǝmǝ ebubǝ lǝ diyi lɔni nǝ ɔnlɛ wǝ kusu bobe ku lefe nǝ unnyi.

ref: This servant will then be punished and thrown out with the ones who only pretended to serve their master. There they will cry and grit their teeth in pain.
text: Onamute ǝmǝ ebunǝ wǝ koto sitinti. Nya wuunǝ wǝ koto kekleke fe kase ińyǝ itǝ ǝnǝmi ǝnǝmi eto betidi seka! Mfó nya wuuwi keku wɔɔyimi animi!

ref: The foolish ones took their lamps, but no extra oil.
text: Ɛnɔ ba leeninǝ étsyi kakandiɛ, fɛɛ béntsyi nnuǝbi lǝ asa ǝsuǝ báwunisǝ.

ref: The ones who were wise took along extra oil for their lamps.
text: Nya babesibe bamǝ alɛɛ, mǝ étsyi nnuǝbi báwunsǝ lǝ mǝ asa ǝsuǝ ku kakandiɛ kamǝ.

ref: Then in the middle of the night someone shouted, “Here's the groom! Come to meet him!”
text: “Ntsyenti lefee, bánɔ kudu bǝnkǝ, ‘Nnyǝ okafɔse ǝmǝ nlɛ bubǝ! Betaka, bibǝ betsyako wǝ!’

ref: When the women got up and started getting their lamps ready,
text: “Mfó nya mbitɛ bomǝ létaka nya bábesǝ mǝ kakandiɛ eto ute kato.

ref: the foolish ones said to the others, “Let us have some of your oil! Our lamps are going out.”
text: Nya mba leeninǝ lɛtɛyi babesibe bamǝ bǝ, ‘Bitǝ bo mi nnuǝbi bomǝ eto ntsyuǝ itsyise bo kakandiɛ lɛ bɔni.’

ref: Those who were wise answered, “There's not enough oil for all of us! Go and buy some for yourselves.”
text: “Nya babesibe bamǝ lɛtɛyi mǝ bǝnkǝ, ‘Bo nnuǝbi emboyo itǝ bo ku mi, nyaso bisu mba laate ɔflɔ, lǝ bisu beya butsyuǝ bitǝ ǝsuǝ.’

ref: But the groom replied, “I don't even know you!”
text: “Nya okafɔse ǝmǝ lɛtɛyi mǝ nkǝ, ‘Kulefe, minnyi mi!’ ”

ref: So, my disciples, always be ready! You don't know the day or the time when all this will happen. A Story about Three Servants (Luke
text: Mfó nya Yesu lélosǝ ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Nyaso bimuǝ ǝsuǝ bitsyuǝ, itsyise binyi diyi nye lefe nǝ muubǝ. Ele Bǝ Bɔlɛ Asa Nnya Onanto Dífi Ókpe Bo Lǝ Ani Kukɔnɔ.

ref: Some time later the master of those servants returned. He called them in and asked what they had done with his money.
text: “Lǝ lefe kɛntsye eto kamaa, beyifoyifo bamfo eto Onamute ǝmǝ ésinkli úbǝ nya údufǝ lǝ nkunta ku mǝ.

ref: You could have at least put my money in the bank, so I could have earned interest on it.”
text: Nyalɛ beso fémemfi mɔ atabi ésuko atabi kǝtsyǝkɔ bǝ lǝ mbǝǝ, lǝ nnyǝ nnya kutǝkǝ?’

ref: The kingdom is also like what happened when a man went away and put his three servants in charge of all he owned.
text: “Lefe nǝmfóo, Onanto eto sekakedikɔ ebutsyǝ fe mmǝ. Lefe lɔnii, Osani utsyuǝ akɛ, wǝ léla nkǝ lǝ usu kusu kutsyuǝ nyaso ókpadi wǝ beyifoyifo nya úfi wǝ bikǝ ókpe mǝ lǝ ani.

ref: As soon as the man had gone, the servant with the
text: Uyifoyifo wǝ léfo atabi eto akpi anɔɔ, ésu ɔ́tsyi ɔ́wuninsǝ lǝ nnya ǝsuǝ nya únyǝ akpi anɔ útǝkǝsǝ.

ref: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
text: “Wǝ Onamute ǝmǝ átɛyi wǝ nkǝ, ‘Alɔ éyifo, uyifoyifo sɛɛ. Éyifo asa lǝ kusu ǝsuǝ nya fálɛ asa sɛkɛ ola, nyaso muufi fǝ ntsyǝ lǝ asa kpǝ eto kɛnyɛ. Dufǝ ǝbǝ lǝ ǝbǝ ǝnyǝnko mɛ disuǝyuǝ!’

ref: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
text: “Wǝ Onamute ǝmǝ átɛyi wǝ nkǝ, ‘Uyifoyifo sɛɛ, éyifo lesa saa lǝ kusu ǝsuǝ tinti! Se fálɛ asa sɛkɛ ola lǝ kusu ǝsuǝ esoo, muufi fǝ ntsyǝ lǝ kpǝ eto kɛnyɛ. Bǝ lǝ ǝbǝ ǝnyǝnko mɛ disuǝyuǝ!’

ref: I was frightened and went out and hid your money in the ground. Here is every single coin!”
text: Nya se mínte sikpi esoo, mfi fǝ atabi nyamǝ nya mínku dibiǝ mímfi nnya mínkpe. Nyaso nyǝ fǝ atabi ni nyamfo.’

ref: The master of the servant told him, “You are lazy and good-for-nothing! You know I harvest what I don't plant and gather crops where I haven't scattered seed.
text: “Wǝ Onamute ǝmǝ átɛyi wǝ nkǝ, ‘Fǝ uyifoyifo bua, ɔflɔ́. Eyi fǝ meefi lesa kaka lǝ mánkpe nya mǝǝfǝsǝ asa lǝ kaka mánto kulesa.

ref: Then the master said, “Now your money will be taken away and given to the servant with
text: “Mfó nya onamute ǝmǝ lɛtɛyi nkǝ, ‘Mɔmɔ, lɛ atabi nyamfo afo lǝ wǝ ani lǝ befi nnya bitǝ nwǝ nkpe akpi lefosi.’

ref: Everyone who has something will be given more, and they will have more than enough. But everything will be taken from those who don't have anything.
text: Itsyise utidi wǝ nkpe asaa, wǝ ke bonsi boofi ditsyuǝ bakpe bawunsǝ nɛ. Nya utidi wǝ kulesa lentee, sɛkɛɛ nte wǝ tsyaa, bɔɔlɛ ni bafo wǝ lǝ ani.

ref: You are a worthless servant, and you will be thrown out into the dark where people will cry and grit their teeth in pain.” The Final Judgment
text: Nya uyifoyifo bimba ǝmfo alɛɛ, beto wǝ bekpe lǝ ketuntu ka nkpe lǝ lekple. Kaka mfóo, wuwi keku unǝ wǝ animi. Utidi Eto Ubi Ebokponko Kawunsiǝ Eto Betidi Lekpo.

ref: When the Son of Man comes in his glory with all his angels, he will sit on his royal throne.
text: “Lefe nǝ Utidi Eto Ubi ǝmǝ luubǝ lǝ wǝ diyǝnde ǝsuǝ ku Onanto eto batɔnkpe kenkee, wuusiǝ lǝ wǝ lekankpomɛ ǝsuǝ.

ref: The people of all nations will be brought before him, and he will separate them, as shepherds separate their sheep from their goats.
text: Nya kawunsiǝ eto ese eto betidi kenke lɔɔbɔ bǝńyǝ lǝ wǝ ǝnǝmi. Nya wuusǝnsǝ mǝ lǝ akpa ǝnuǝ fe kase nwǝ laayɛnko bebuki leesǝnsǝ basamu kǝlǝkǝ lǝ bate nti.

ref: He will place the sheep on his right and the goats on his left.
text: Wuuńyǝsǝ betidi sɛɛ lǝ wǝ kodiani, nya wuutǝ mba ni betidi bua ńyǝ lǝ wǝ kǝmǝni.

ref: Then the king will say to those on his right, “My father has blessed you! Come and receive the kingdom that was prepared for you before the world was created.
text: “Nya Oka ǝmǝ lɔɔtɛyi mba ńyǝ wǝ lǝ kodiani nkǝ, ‘Bibǝ, mi mba mɔ anto lékpo atsyɔ! Bibǝ bidufǝ lǝ sekakedikɔ ka bálɔ bǝtsyǝ itǝ mi idu taa kawunsiǝ eto ketsyikokɔ ke.

ref: When I was hungry, you gave me something to eat, and when I was thirsty, you gave me something to drink. When I was a stranger, you welcomed me,
text: Kukpɛnsɛ álɛ mɛ nya bítǝ mɛ lesa míndi, utukuɛ álɛ mɛ, nya bítǝ mɛ ntu mínnǝ. Nyifo ɔfɔ nya béfo mɛ békpe lǝ mi ayo.

ref: and when I was naked, you gave me clothes to wear. When I was sick, you took care of me, and when I was in jail, you visited me.”
text: Lefe nǝ mínyɛ leyaa, bítǝ mɛ kafia nti. Nya lefe nǝ mínlɛ bufii, bɛlɛ mɛ ola. Lefe nǝ bákpe mɛ lǝ diyo, bíbǝ bebe mɛ.’

ref: Then the ones who pleased the Lord will ask, “When did we give you something to eat or drink?
text: “Mfó nya betidi sɛɛ bamǝ lɔɔtɔ wǝ bǝnkǝ, ‘Bonamute, dimanfe búnyǝ kukpɛnsɛ nlɛ fǝ fɛ bútǝ lesa fédi, nye bútǝ fǝ ntu fǝnǝ?

ref: When did we welcome you as a stranger or give you clothes to wear
text: Dimanfe búnyǝ fǝ ɔfɔ fɛ bófo fǝ nye fányɛ leya bútǝ fǝ kafia féti?

ref: or visit you while you were sick or in jail?”
text: Dimanfe bɔ́nɔ bǝ alɛ bufi nye bǝ akɛ lǝ diyo fɛ búbǝ bóbe fǝ?’

ref: The king will answer, “Whenever you did it for any of my people, no matter how unimportant they seemed, you did it for me.”
text: “Mfó nya Oka ǝmǝ luutǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, ‘Nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi minkǝ, lefe saa nǝ fáwunsǝko utsyuǝ wǝ lǝmbǝ dibu saa lǝ mɔ bayimi bamfo ntii, mɔ féyifo fǝtǝ.’

ref: Then the king will say to those on his left, “Get away from me! You are under God's curse. Go into the everlasting fire prepared for the devil and his angels!
text: “Mfó wɔɔtɛyi mba ńyǝ lǝ wǝ kǝmǝni nkǝ, ‘Bidu mɛ lǝ ɔflɔ, mi mba Onanto díkudi! Bisu bidufǝ lǝ ditefɛnku manlo nǝ Onanto létsyaka útǝ Obonsam ku wǝ batɔnkpe!

ref: I was hungry, but you did not give me anything to eat, and I was thirsty, but you did not give me anything to drink.
text: Kukpɛnsɛ álɛ mɛ, fɛɛ bɛni bíǝntǝ mɛ lesa ndi. Utukuɛ álɛ mɛ, fɛɛ bíǝntǝ mɛ ntu nko ke.

ref: I was a stranger, but you did not welcome me, and I was naked, but you did not give me any clothes to wear. I was sick and in jail, but you did not take care of me.”
text: Nyifo ɔfɔ wǝ díbǝ ɔ́sɔnɔ mi, tsya bíamfo mɛ. Nyɛ leya tsya bíenti mɛ kafia. Nnɔ bufi, bákpe mɛ lǝ diyo, fɛɛ bíǝmbǝ bisiǝsǝ mɛ.’

ref: Then the people will ask, “Lord, when did we fail to help you when you were hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in jail?”
text: “Mfó buutǝ wǝ mbuayɛ bǝnkǝ, ‘Boanto, lemanfe búnyǝ kukpɛnsɛ lɛlɛ fǝ, nye ututkuɛ lɛ fǝ, nye féyifo ɔfɔ, nye fányɛ leya, nye fanlɛ bufi, nye fakɛ lǝ diyo fɛ bóǝmbǝ bobe nye bɔ́wunsǝko fǝ?’

ref: The king will say to them, “Whenever you failed to help any of my people, no matter how unimportant they seemed, you failed to do it for me.”
text: “Oka ǝmǝ ebutǝ mbuayɛ nkǝ, ‘Nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi minkǝ, lefe nǝ eto lefe bíenyifo kasɛɛ bítǝ betidi ba bíembu kulesa lǝ mɔ bǝtsyuǝ bamfo ntii, mɔ uni bíenyifo kulesa bitǝ!’ ”

ref: Then Jesus said, “Those people will be punished forever. But the ones who pleased God will have eternal life.”
text: Yesu átɛyi ólosǝ nkǝ, “Nyaso boofi mba mfo batsyese kanyi kǝnyǝkɔ itǝ manlo manlo. Nya betidi sɛɛ bamfóo, mǝ ebunyǝ nkpǝ bo lǝmbɔkɛ kalokɔ kulefe kulefe.”

ref: “You know two days from now will be Passover. This is when the Son of Man will be handed over to his enemies and nailed to a cross.”
text: “Iyifo mi bote bǝnkǝ ibu eyi ǝnuǝ lǝ Ukpǝ Asa Bo Ɔ́yila eto Diyi nǝmǝ lǝ leyoo, nya boofi Utidi Eto Ubi bǝtǝ bǝ basɔ wǝ bamanklasǝ lǝ kuyi ǝsuǝ.”

ref: Finally, Jesus returned to his disciples and said, “Are you still sleeping and resting? The time has come for the Son of Man to be handed over to sinners.
text: Nya úsinkli úbǝ bakasebi bamǝ ɔflɔ nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Bisi belabe selabe bɛlɛ ɔnwɛ butsyǝ? Bebe! Lefe nǝmǝ eboyo bǝ befi Utidi Eto Ubi ǝmǝ bakpe lǝ betidi bua eto ani.

ref: They planned how they could sneak around and have Jesus arrested and put to death.
text: Befi nsusu bǝ boomuǝ Yesu kalɔkalɔ lǝ balo wǝ.

ref: But they said, “We must not do it during Passover, because the people will riot.” At Bethany (Mark
text: Bátɛyi tsya bǝnkǝ, “Yanle bǝ bumuǝ wǝ lǝ diyi nǝmǝ budi kǝmiǝ, lenni nko betidi bamǝ ebunyǝnki bataka bǝńyǝ bo lǝ ǝsuǝ.” Usio Utsyuǝ Édiki Yesu Nnuǝbi Fifi Lǝ Betania.

ref: We could have sold this perfume for a lot of money and given it to the poor.”
text: Boofo nnuǝbi bomfó boté itǝ koya kplɛ, befi atabi nyamǝ bǝtǝ bakombo!”

ref: But when his disciples saw this, they became angry and complained, “Why such a waste?
