text: Lǝ wǝ disi katoo, báwɔni asa bámanklasǝ lǝ uyi ǝmǝ ǝsuǝ. Nnya báwɔni bǝnkǝ lesa nǝ eso bálo wǝ ni bǝ, “Nnyǝ Yesu, Nwǝ Ni Yudafɔ Eto Oka.”

ref: shouted, “So you're the one who claimed you could tear down the temple and build it again in three days! If you are God's Son, save yourself and come down from the cross!”
text: Bátɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Fǝ ni nwǝ lɛtɛyi fǝnkǝ eboba Desumuyo Kplɛ akpo lǝ kasɔ, esinkli ato ni ǝńyǝnsǝ lǝ eyi ǝtsyǝ ǝsuǝ nɛ! Lǝkǝ fǝ ǝsuǝ, lǝ Onanto eto Ubi feni! Yifǝ lǝ uyikabie komǝ ǝsuǝ ǝbǝ kasɔ.”

ref: Others said, “Wait! Let's see if Elijah will come and save him.”
text: Fɛɛ betidi bamba átɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Bińyǝ lǝ bobe! Bitǝ lǝ bobe lǝ Elia ebubǝ ulǝkǝ wǝ utsyiko kuyi komfo ǝsuǝ.”

ref: At noon the sky turned dark and stayed that way until three o'clock.
text: Idu lǝ kakɔmi eto abombo lefosi ǝnuǝǝ, kawunsiǝ kenke ána tũũ isu ese lǝ ketsyitsyokɔmi eto abombo ǝtsyǝ.

ref: Then about that time Jesus shouted, “Eli, Eli, lema sabachthani?” which means, “My God, my God, why have you deserted me?”
text: Fe abombo ǝtsyǝ etii, Yesu áfa bembembe ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Eli, Eli, lama sabaktani?” Nyamfo eto kasɔ ni, “Mɔ Onanto, mɔ Onanto! Beso fáyani mɛ fǝtsyǝ?”

ref: One of them at once ran and grabbed a sponge. He soaked it in wine, then put it on a stick and held it up to Jesus.
text: Mǝ ɔni étsyetsyi nya úfi okosa wǝ bákpe lǝ nte eto sonkpe, nya úfi mu ku uyi úsuko nkǝ lǝ utǝ wǝ lǝ unǝ.

ref: At once the curtain in the temple was torn in two from top to bottom. The earth shook, and rocks split apart.
text: Lamfolamfoo, Disumuyo Kplɛ eto kafia kplɛ dísie lǝ akpa ǝnuǝ itsyi kato ibǝ kasɔ. Nya kasɔ kamǝ kenke dítinkǝ, nya aba léba.

ref: Graves opened, and many of God's people were raised to life.
text: Edi ásini, nya Onanto eto betidi kɛnkɛ kpǝ ba díkekpǝ létaka bǝbǝ nkpǝ.

ref: They left their graves, and after Jesus had risen to life, they went into the holy city, where they were seen by many people.
text: Bábɔ lǝ mǝ edi kafo nya se Yesu létaka útsyi ɔkɔmǝ úbǝ nkpǝǝ, bésu Yudafɔ eto Umǝ Kɛnkɛ ǝmǝ kafo kaka betidi kpǝ dínyǝ mǝ.

ref: The officer and the soldiers guarding Jesus felt the earthquake and saw everything else that happened. They were frightened and said, “This man really was God's Son!”
text: Se Omamanyǝ ninǝ ku bamamanyǝ ba díńyǝ bákase Yesu dínyǝ kase kasɔ dítinkǝ ku asa nnya díbǝ mfó kenkee, sikpi ǝmuǝ mǝ tinti! Nya bátɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Sitintii, utidi ǝmfo éyifo Onanto eto Ubi.”

ref: Many women had come with Jesus from Galilee to be of help to him, and they were there, looking on at a distance.
text: Besio kpǝ ba dítsyi Galilea ba díkawunsǝko Yesu ǝńyǝ lǝ kɔsɛsɛ bákabe asa nnya díbǝ.

ref: That evening a rich disciple named Joseph from the town of Arimathea
text: Se éyo ditsyitsyo lefee, otabinyǝ utsyuǝ wǝ baakpo Yosef, nya úyifo Yesu eto okasebi wǝ ditsyi Arimatea ǝbǝ.

ref: went and asked for Jesus' body. Pilate gave orders for it to be given to Joseph,
text: Úsu Pilato ɔflɔ nya ɔ́tɔ wǝ nkǝ lǝ ofo Yesu eto ɔkɔ. Pilato ǝtǝ kusu nkǝ befi ɔkɔ ǝmǝ bǝtǝ Yosef.

ref: who took the body and wrapped it in a clean linen cloth.
text: Nyaso Yosef áfo ɔkɔ ǝmǝ nya úfi kafia flubia kǝtsyuǝ úmli wǝ.

ref: Then Joseph put the body in his own tomb that had been cut into solid rock and had never been used. He rolled a big stone against the entrance to the tomb and went away.
text: Nya úfi wǝ úsu úbikǝ lǝ kebikekɔ lǝ wǝ kosate eto kudi fɔfɔ ko bǝlǝkǝ lǝ koba kǝmiǝ. Nya ominansǝ leba nǝ lɛmɔ úti ko kɛnyɛ nya údu.

ref: All this time Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were sitting across from the tomb.
text: Maria Magdalene ku Maria nuǝfǝ ǝsiǝ mfó balɛ kudi komǝ bobe. Bamamanyǝ Áse Yesu Eto Kudi.

ref: On the next day, which was a Sabbath, the chief priests and the Pharisees went together to Pilate.
text: Nya se kale disiǝ nya ini Ɔnwɛditsyǝyii esoo, usumunyǝ ninǝ ku Farisifɔ ésu Pilato ɔflɔ.

ref: They said, “Sir, we remember what this liar said while he was still alive. He claimed in three days he would come back from death.
text: Nya bátɛyi wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Uninǝ, lefe nǝ odiantidi ǝmǝ dísiǝ nkpǝǝ, ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, ‘Lǝ nkpǝǝ, bootakasǝ mɛ betsyiko ɔkɔmǝ lǝ eyi ǝtsyǝ eto kama.’

ref: So please order the tomb to be carefully guarded for three days. If you don't, his disciples may come and steal his body. They will tell the people he has been raised to life, and this last lie will be worse than the first one.”
text: Nyaso tǝ lǝ bamamanyǝ lǝ base kudi komǝ kukɔnɔ isu ese diyi tsyǝfǝ nǝmǝ, kase wǝ bakasebi lembusu beyu ɔkɔ ǝmǝ bedunko batɛyi betidi bǝnkǝ ótaka utsyi ɔkɔmǝ. Lǝ ibǝ nkoo, sidia sia bɔɔtɛyi ǝbɔkɛ seso nsia kɔ.”

ref: Pilate said to them, “All right, take some of your soldiers and guard the tomb as well as you know how.”
text: Mfó nya Pilato lɛtɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Bitǝ lǝ bamamanyǝ lǝ besu base kudi komǝ fe kase boofo.”

ref: Jesus' eleven disciples went to a mountain in Galilee, where Jesus had told them to meet him.
text: Lǝ nnya eto kamaa, bakasebi lefosi ɔnwɛ bamǝ ábe bésu kɔtini ko nkpe lǝ Galilea ǝsuǝ kaka Yesu lɛtɛyi mǝ utsyǝ.

ref: So the chief priests met with the leaders and decided to bribe the soldiers with a lot of money.
text: Besumunyǝ ninǝ bamǝ ku umǝ eto beninǝ ákpe disi béfi nsusu. Bǝtǝ bamamanyǝ bamǝ atabi eto akplɛ,

ref: and teach them to do everything I have told you. I will be with you always, even until the end of the world.
text: nya bete mǝ bǝ betsyue lesa saa nǝ mínte mi koto. Nya lǝ sitintii, mɔɔkɛ ku mi lefe saa, isu ese lǝ kawunsiǝ eto kalosǝkɔ.”

ref: Then Jesus said, “Don't be afraid! Tell my followers to go to Galilee. They will see me there.” Report of the Guard
text: Yesu átɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Biante sikpi. Bisu bɛtɛyi mɔ bayimi biǝ besu Galilea, mfó buunyǝ mɛ nɛ.” Bamamanyǝ Ba Lése Kudi Komɛ Átɛyi Lesa Nǝ Bǝnyǝ.

ref: Suddenly a strong earthquake struck, and the Lord's angel came down from heaven. He rolled away the stone and sat on it.
text: Lamfolamfoo, kasɔ étinkǝ kekleke. Onanto eto Ɔtɔnkpe utsyuǝ áyifǝ utsyi kato úbǝ ɔ́minansǝ leba kplɛ nǝmǝ nya úsiǝ lǝ ni ǝsuǝ.

ref: The angel looked as bright as lightning, and his clothes were white as snow.
text: Wǝ ditsyǝbi ékeyǝnde fe kase ɔsi laato nya wǝ awu diflubi futututu fe aklala seka.

ref: The guards shook from fear and fell down, as though they were dead.
text: Sikpi ǝmuǝ mba nlɛ mfó bose nya bétinkǝ tinti, nya bákpo kasɔ mfó fe bukpǝ békpe.

ref: The angel said to the women, “Don't be afraid! I know you are looking for Jesus, who was nailed to a cross.
text: Onanto eto Ɔtɔnkpe ǝmfó édinko besio bamǝ etiki nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Biante sikpi! Nnyi miǝ Yesu wǝ básɔ bámanklasǝ lǝ kuyi ǝsuǝ bɛnlɛ bɔbɛbɛ.

ref: Now hurry! Tell his disciples he has been raised to life and is on his way to Galilee. Go there, and you will see him. This is what I came to tell you.”
text: Mɔmɔɔ, bisu mlǝ lǝ bisu bɛtɛyi wǝ bakasebi biǝ, ‘Bátakasǝ wǝ betsyiko ɔkɔmǝ úbǝ nkpǝ, nya ɔntɔ mi sɛtɔ úsu Galilea. Mfó biunyǝ wǝ nɛ.’ Bekloma nnya míntɛyi mi mfo.”

ref: Suddenly Jesus met them and greeted them. They went near him, held on to his feet, and worshiped him.
text: Lamfolamfoo, Yesu átsyako mǝ, nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Diyuǝ itǝ mi.” Báyɛ bétu wǝ bábokosǝ ǝsuǝ kasɔ bákɔbe wǝ lǝ akpa nya bésumu wǝ.

ref: While the women were on their way, some soldiers who had been guarding the tomb went into the city. They told the chief priests everything that had happened.
text: Lefe nǝ besio bamfó dísinkli bantɔɔ, bamamanyǝ ba lése kudi komǝ ésu umǝ nya bátɛyi besumunyǝ ninǝ bamǝ asa nnya kenke díbǝ mfó.

ref: They said to the soldiers, “Tell everyone that Jesus' disciples came during the night and stole his body while you were asleep.
text: nya bátɛyi mǝ bǝnkǝ, “Bɛtɛyi biǝnkǝ lefe nǝ bélabe selabe ku ketsyee, wǝ bakasebi ǝbǝ béyu wǝ ɔkɔ ǝmǝ bédunko.

ref: The soldiers took the money and did what they were told. The people of Judea still tell each other this story. What Jesus' Followers Must Do (Mark
text: Bamamanyǝ bamǝ áfo atabi nyamǝ nya béyifo fe kase bátɛyi mǝ. Nyaso nyamfo ni ditiki nǝ lɛyɛ létsyilama Yudafɔ ntii taa mmi nɛ. Yesu Ǝlǝkǝ Ǝsuǝ Óte Wǝ Bakasebi.

ref: Jesus came to them and said: I have been given all authority in heaven and on earth!
text: Yesu ányi ɔfi mǝ nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Onanto éfi kato ku kasɔ kenke eto ǝsuǝale útǝ mɛ.

ref: Go to the people of all nations and make them my disciples. Baptize them in the name of the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit,
text: Nyaso bisu kawunsiǝ eto betidi kenke ɔflɔ lǝ biyifo mǝ mɔ bakasebi. Biflǝsǝ mǝ ntu lǝ Anto ku Ubi ku Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛ eto diye ǝsuǝ,

ref: They saw him and worshiped him, but some of them doubted.
text: Se bǝnyǝ wǝǝ, bésumu wǝ, ite bǝ mǝ bǝtsyuǝ áyaki tsya.

ref: Right then the two brothers dropped their nets and went with him.
text: Lamfolamfo báyani mǝ asabu bǝtsyǝ nya bátaka bǝtǝkǝko wǝ.

ref: Jesus walked on and soon saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat, mending their nets.
text: Ɔ́yɛ úsu sɛtɔ sɛkɛ nya únyǝ bayimisani inuǝ bǝtsyuǝ ba ni Sebedeo eto bebi ba ni Yakobo ku Yohanes. Besi mǝ ɔklɔ kafo balɛ mǝ asabu bɔlɔ.

ref: News about Jesus quickly spread all over Galilee. Jesus Heals Many People (Matthew
text: Itǝ esoo, Yesu eto diye áyɛ letsyo lǝ Galilea eto kasɔ kenke. Yesu Átsya Betidi Kpǝ Bufi.

ref: It began just as God had said in the book written by Isaiah the prophet, “I am sending my messenger to get the way ready for you.
text: Lǝ lefe kɛntsyɛ nǝ léfe mǝǝ, Onanto eto unyǝlǝkǝtidi Yesaya áwɔni utsyǝ nkǝ Onanto átɛyi wǝ ubi nkǝ, “Mootsyese ɔtɔyɛ ntɔnsǝ. Wǝ lɔɔlɔ betidi utsyǝ itǝ fǝ bubǝ.

ref: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
text: Yohanes ákpe ɔflimu eto bisǝ eto awudindi nya úfi kubukikuǝ eto abɔsu ókle wǝ nkɛ. Didisa nǝ úkǝdi didi bablɔ ku bǝkpǝsǝ.

ref: John also told the people, “Someone more powerful is going to come. And I am not good enough even to stoop down and untie his sandals.
text: Wǝ etiki nnya ɔnlɛ ditsya bobe ni nkǝ, “Utidi wǝ ntǝkǝ mɛ kama ɔnlɛ bubǝǝ, so mɛ famfamfa nya yǝńyǝ itǝ bǝ ńyǝ lǝ wǝ kɛnyɛ nyifo wǝ keyifo blɛmblɛ.

ref: Jesus and his disciples went to the town of Capernaum. Then on the next Sabbath he went into the synagogue and started teaching.
text: Yesu ku mba ntǝkǝko wǝ ésu bédufǝ umǝ wǝ baakpo Kapernaum. Nya lǝ Yudafɔ eto Ɔnwɛditsyǝyi nǝ ntǝkǝ lǝ mǝ mfó eto busu ǝsuǝ eto kamaa, Yesu ésu Yudafɔ eto katsyakɔ nya útsyiko mǝ Anantotiki bote.

ref: Everyone was amazed at his teaching. He taught with authority, and not like the teachers of the Law of Moses.
text: Iyifo mba kenke lɛnɔ wǝ asatete nyamǝ yanii, itsyise ɛbɔ lete lǝ mba laate asa itsyi lǝ Yudafɔ eto afi ǝsuǝ. Bomu wǝ asatete eyifo nnya nkpe kalende nya itsyi taa wǝ utu kǝmiǝ.

ref: About that time Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
text: Yanklɛ fɛ Yesu ditsyi umǝ wǝ baakpo Nasaret lǝ Galilea eto kasɔ, ubǝ udufǝ, nya Yohanes díyifo wǝ ntuflǝsǝflǝsǝ eto lekpa lǝ Yordan.

ref: As soon as Jesus came out of the water, he saw the sky open and the Holy Spirit coming down to him like a dove.
text: Lǝ kase Yesu nlɛ bɔbɔ lǝ ntu bomǝ ǝsuǝ kabe katoo, únyǝ nkǝ Onanto eto kato lɛ bɔsini nya Onanto eto Lɛwɔnɔ lɛyifǝ dísiǝ lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ lǝ lɛblɛkpa eto ditsyǝbi ǝsuǝ.

ref: A man with leprosy came to Jesus and knelt down. He begged, “You have the power to make me well, if only you wanted to.”
text: Osani utsyuǝ wǝ nlɛ bufisia bufi ǝbǝ Yesu ɔflɔ. Óse akonki ɔ́lɛ wǝ lekpakpa nkǝ lǝ ɔwunsǝko wǝ. Ɔ́tɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Lǝ alaa, ebofo mɛ botsya nya yuuyuǝ mɛ tsya.”

ref: He said, “The time has come! God's kingdom will soon be here. Turn back to God and believe the good news!” Jesus Chooses Four Fishermen (Matthew
text: Ɔkɛ mfó katɛyi nkǝ, “Lefe sɛɛ nǝmǝ eboyo. Onanto eto sekakedikɔ eto lefe ǝbɔfi. Bisinkli lǝ mi asa bua eto kama lǝ befo Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmfo bɛnɔ.”

ref: At once Jesus asked them to come with him. They left their father in the boat with the hired workers and went with him. A Man with an Evil Spirit (Luke
text: Kase Yesu dínyǝ mǝ koo, ókpadi mǝ nkǝ bǝtǝkǝko wǝ. Mǝ tsya átaka akpa akpa báyani mǝ anto Sebedeo ku wǝ beyifoyifo bǝtsyǝ lǝ ɔklɔ ǝmǝ kafo bǝtǝkǝko Yesu. Yesu Ála Lɛwɔnɔ Bua Lǝ Osani Utsyuǝ Ǝsuǝ.

ref: Jesus went to her. He took hold of her hand and helped her up. The fever left her, and she served them a meal.
text: Nyaso Yesu ésu wǝ ɔflɔ, nya ɔ́kɔbe wǝ lǝ kɔni ótakasǝ úńyǝnsǝ. Lamfolamfo wǝ bufi bomǝ áyɛ wǝ nya ótaka útǝ mɛ lesa bédi.

ref: And when they found him, they said, “Everyone is looking for you!”
text: Lǝ lefe nǝ bǝnyǝ wǝǝ, bátɛyi wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Utsyuǝ saa ǝbǝ ɔlɛ fǝ bɔbɛbɛ.”

ref: Suddenly a man with an evil spirit in him entered the synagogue and yelled,
text: Lǝ lefe nǝmǝ tutuutuu, osani utsyuǝ lɛwɔnɔ bua nsi wǝ lǝ ǝsuǝ akɛ lǝ katsyakɔ kamǝ mfó,

ref: “Jesus from Nazareth, what do you want with us? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are! You are God's Holy One.”
text: nya ófa bembembe ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Yesu Nasaretnyǝ, be fanlɛ bɔbɛbɛ ketsyiko lǝ bo ɔflɔ mfo? Bubǝ fǝbǝ fǝ ǝǝbǝ atsylɔsǝ bo lee? Nyi futsyǝ feni. Fǝ ni Onanto eto Ɔkɛnkɛnyǝ wǝ ótsyese nɛ.”

ref: Jesus told the evil spirit, “Be quiet and come out of the man!”
text: Yesu éti lɛwɔnɔ nǝmǝ kusu nya ókpe ni kɔfi nkǝ, “Dika, nya lǝ abɔ lǝ osani ǝmǝ ǝsuǝ.”

ref: The spirit shook him. Then it gave a loud shout and left.
text: Lɛwɔnɔ nǝmfo étinkǝsǝ osani ǝmǝ keklekle nya dítǝ ófa bǝmbǝǝmbǝ fɛ lɛbɔ lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ.

ref: Everyone was completely surprised and kept saying to each other, “What is this? It must be some new kind of powerful teaching! Even the evil spirits obey him.”
text: Itǝ iyifo mba kenke díńyǝ mfó yanii, nya bátɔ mǝ ǝsuǝ bǝnkǝ, “Nte asatete fɔfɔ nnya eto okle nɛ? Utidi ǝmfo kpe ǝsuǝale woafo etiki budi ku awɔnɔ bua nya beetsyue wǝ etiki ato.”

ref: Jesus replied, “We must go to the nearby towns, so that I can tell the good news to those people. This is why I have come.”
text: Fɛɛ Yesu átɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Bitǝ lǝ busu umǝ bamba. Efe bǝ ndi Anantotiki lǝ mfó tsya, itsyise nyamfo ni mɔ keyifo ka mimbǝ keyifokɔ lǝ kawunsiǝ nɛ.”

ref: Jesus felt sorry for the man. So he put his hand on him and said, “I want to! Now you are well.”
text: Wǝ etiki nyamǝ ǝnǝ Yesu kafo, nyaso únǝ wǝ kɔni nya útidi wǝ. Nya ɔ́tɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Nla nko. Fǝ ǝsuǝ kenke lǝ yele.”

ref: As soon as Jesus left the synagogue with James and John, they went home with Simon and Andrew.
text: Yesu ku wǝ bakasebi ku Yakobo ku Yohanes édu lǝ katsyakɔ kamǝ nya bésu Simon ku Andreas eto diyo.

ref: When they got there, Jesus was told that Simon's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
text: Se bésu bédufǝ mfóo, betidi bǝtsyuǝ átɛyi Yesu bǝnkǝ, Simon eto usiǝambe áyila, atikesi ǝmuǝ wǝ olabe.

ref: That evening after sunset, all who were sick or had demons in them were brought to Jesus.
text: Se ditsyitsyo lefe léyoo, betidi ákpa befimfi kenke ku mba awɔnɔ bua díkakpe bulǝ kpǝ báboko Yesu bǝ lǝ otsya mǝ bufi.

ref: In fact, the whole town gathered around the door of the house.
text: Nya umǝ ǝmǝ kenke eto betidi díbǝ bǝńyǝ lǝ kesie mfó bánlɛ bobe.

ref: Jesus healed all kinds of terrible diseases and forced out a lot of demons. But the demons knew who he was, and he did not let them speak.
text: Yesu átsya betidi kpǝ ba nlɛ bufi ateate bufi nya óla awɔnɔ bua lǝ betidi kpǝ ǝsuǝ ku mba awɔnɔ bua nsi lǝ ǝsuǝ, itsyise Yesu yi nkǝ awɔnɔ bua nyamǝ yi futsyǝ uni, nyaso wántɔnɔ nkǝ awɔnɔ nyamǝ tɛyi kulesa itsyi lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ. Yesu Ábe Ditiki Sɛɛ Nǝnǝ Letsya Lǝ Galilea Eto Kasɔ.

ref: Very early the next morning before daylight, Jesus got up and went to a place where he could be alone and pray.
text: Kolensiǝnto fɛ kale luusiǝǝ, Yesu átaka ɔ́bɔ lǝ diyo nǝmǝ. Úsu kaka kutsyuǝ saa lǝmbǝ nya óto ola útǝ Onanto.

ref: Simon and the others started looking for him.
text: Kamaa, Simon ku wǝ bayɛtsyuǝ ésu bábɛbɛ Yesu.

ref: Then Jesus went to their synagogues everywhere in Galilee, where he preached and forced out demons. Jesus Heals a Man (Matthew
text: Nyaso ɔ́yɛ ótsyilama Galilea eto kasɔ kenke, nya úkedi Anantotiki nyamǝ lǝ Yudafɔ eto ntsyakɔ kpǝ nya óla awɔnɔ bua ɔ́bɔsǝ lǝ betidi ǝsuǝ tsya. Yesu Átsya Osani Utsyuǝ Wǝ Nlɛ Bufisia Bufi.

ref: At once the man's leprosy disappeared, and he was well.
text: Lamfolamfo bufi bomǝ ǝyuǝ wǝ.

ref: After Jesus strictly warned the man, he sent him on his way.
text: Mfó nya Yesu lɛtɛyi wǝ nkǝ udu, nya ókpe wǝ kɔfi keklekle nkǝ,

ref: He said, “Don't tell anyone about this. Just go and show the priest that you are well. Then take a gift to the temple as Moses commanded, and everyone will know that you have been healed.”
text: “Mǝnńyǝ lǝ kusu atɛyi betidi nnya dibǝ, bomu su lǝ efi fǝ ǝsuǝ esu ate Onanto eto Usumunyǝ. Lǝ efi nkpata bo Mose eto kɔfi lɛtɔ esuko. Nyamfo luulǝkǝ ete bǝnkǝ bufi bomǝ ǝyuǝ fǝ nɛ.”

ref: The man talked about it so much and told so many people, that Jesus could no longer go openly into a town. He had to stay away from the towns, but people still came to him from everywhere.
text: Tsya lefe nǝ nwǝ ǝmǝ dídu ɔntɔɔ, útsyiko bofa katɛyi kase Yesu létsya wǝ. Lǝ nnya esoo, Yesu ménsifo umǝ saa busu, itsyise betidi kpǝ lɛ wǝ ɔflɔ bubǝ. Nyamfo esoo, Yesu kosate átsyimɛ lǝ umǝmflɔ kaka kuutsyǝ lǝmbǝ. Nya betidi ditsyi nlebe nlebe kpǝ, bǝbǝ wǝ ɔflɔ mfó.

ref: Then so many of them came to the house that there wasn't even standing room left in front of the door. Jesus was still teaching
text: Nya betidi kpǝ díbǝ diyo nǝ ɔkɛ, ku ni kayoma kenke. Nya ɔkɛ mfó úkedi mǝ etiki.

ref: when four people came up, carrying a man on a mat because he could not walk.
text: Mfó nya betidi ena léboko ufimfi wǝ léba onkpo kasɔ wǝ ɔflɔ.

ref: But because of the crowd, they could not get him to Jesus. So they made a hole in the roof above him and let the man down in front of everyone.
text: Nya se banlɛ kusu bunyǝ lǝ bayɛnko wǝ lǝ kesie bedufǝnko wǝ diyo kafo lǝ kase betidi mfui esoo, bésu bábe letsya bǝlǝkǝ ofo nya báyɛnko ufimfi ǝmǝ ku wǝ belabekǝ báyifǝsǝ bǝtsyǝ le Yesu eto akɔ.

ref: “Why would he say such a thing? He must think he is God! Only God can forgive sins.”
text: “Beso woatɛyi nko? Ɔlɛ mbusiotiki budi. Onanto hã laafo abua bufi katsyɛ!”

ref: I will show you that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” So Jesus said to the man,
text: Nyaso lǝ itǝ bǝ befo bɛnɔ biǝ Utidi Eto Ubii, nkpe ǝsuǝale bǝ mfi betidi eto abua ntsyɛ le kawunsiǝ kamfo. Bɛnɔ?” Mfó ǝmǝǝ, Yesu esi útsyi wǝ ǝnǝmi útǝ ufimfi ǝmǝ nya ɔ́tɛyi wǝ nkǝ,

ref: “Get up! Pick up your mat and go on home.”
text: “Taka lǝ efi fǝ belabekǝ lǝ esu diyo.”

ref: Once again, Jesus went to the shore of Lake Galilee. A large crowd gathered around him, and he taught them.
text: Nya Yesu disi údu úsu Galilea eto Ditumbukplɛ kɛnyɛ nya betidi bamǝ kenke díbǝ wǝ ɔflɔ mfó, nya óte mǝ Anantotiki.

ref: Jesus finished by saying, “People were not made for the good of the Sabbath. The Sabbath was made for the good of people.
text: Nya Yesu lɛtɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Onanto ǝtsyǝ Ɔnwɛditsyǝyi itǝ utidi eto lɛlɛkǝsǝ. Inni utidi bǝtsyǝ itǝ diyi nǝmǝ eto lɛlɛkɛsǝ.

ref: Later, Jesus and his disciples were having dinner at Levi's house. Many tax collectors and other sinners had become followers of Jesus, and they were also guests at the dinner.
text: Ketsyitsyokɔmii, Yesu ku wǝ bakasebi ku betidi kpǝ ba ntǝkǝko wǝ, ésu Lewi eto bǝ lǝ besu bedi lesa ku wǝ. Mba dítǝkǝko wǝ eto bǝtsyuǝ éyifo mba laafo umǝnko ku babuayifo.

ref: Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees, and they saw Jesus eating with sinners and tax collectors. So they asked his disciples, “Why does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?”
text: Nya afi eto bateasa ba ni Yudafɔ eto lekpa nǝ baakpo Farisifɔ tsya akɛ mfó. Nyaso lefe nǝ afi eto bateasa ba ni Farisifɔ bamǝ dínyǝ bǝ Yesu si ɔ́lɛ lesa budi ku babuayifo, bátɔ wǝ bakasebi bǝnkǝ, “Ntsyǝso wonko mba laafo umǝnko ku babuayifo nsi banlɛ lesa budi?”

ref: The followers of John the Baptist and the Pharisees often went without eating. Some people came and asked Jesus, “Why do the followers of John and those of the Pharisees often go without eating, while your disciples never do?”
text: Lǝ lefe ditsyuǝ se Yohanes ku wǝ bakasebi ku Farisifɔ lɛkɛ lǝ kɛnyɛkekle ǝsuǝǝ, betidi bǝtsyuǝ ǝbǝ Yesu ɔflɔ nya bátɔ wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Beso fɛ fǝ bakasebi laankle kɛnyɛ fe kase Yohanes eto bakasebi ku Farisifɔ laakle?”

ref: Jesus answered: The friends of a bridegroom don't go without eating while he is still with them.
text: Yesu ǝtǝ mǝ mbuayɛ lǝ lekpa seka ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Nyalɛ Okafɔsǝ eto bakloboo, bani lesa budi lǝ kusiǝ kakpakɔ kamǝ? Lee, yanle bǝ bǝnyǝ disuǝyuǝ ku okafɔsǝ ǝmǝ nkpe mǝ lentii?

ref: But the time will come when he will be taken from them. Then they will go without eating.
text: Yuubǝ diyi ditsyuǝ bǝ lǝ bǝlǝkǝ okafɔsǝ ǝmǝ lǝ mǝ nti, baklobo bamǝ ebokle kɛnyɛ beyifo yoyoyo.

ref: No one patches old clothes by sewing on a piece of new cloth. The new piece would shrink and tear a bigger hole.
text: Kuutsyuǝ saa emfi lefianku fɔfɔ kaka kama lǝ kafia koko ka nlɛ bola ǝsuǝ, lefianku fɔfɔ nǝmǝ ebunǝ kafia koko kamǝ kala lǝ bǝflǝ kǝ, nya luutǝ dibiǝ koko nǝmǝ sǝnsǝ lɛmɔ leso kɔ.

ref: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined. New wine must be put into new wineskins. A Question about the Sabbath (Matthew
text: Nnya kamaa, bankpo ntǝ fɔfɔ lǝ kubukikuǝtobe koko wǝ bákakpo ntǝ bǝnyǝ kafo. Ntǝ fɔfɔ bomǝ ebɔkpɔ bobe kato itǝ bǝ kubukikuetobe koko ǝmǝ ebola itǝ bǝ ntǝ ku kubukikuǝtobe ǝmǝ ebuyifo asɔsɔ. Bomu ntǝ fɔfɔɔ, ubukikuǝtobe fɔfɔ ǝsuǝ baakpo mu.” Yesu Ni Ɔnwɛditsyǝyi Eto Onamute.

ref: One Sabbath Jesus and his disciples were walking through some wheat fields. His disciples were picking grains of wheat as they went along.
text: Lefe lɔni lǝ Ɔnwɛditsyǝyii, se Yesu ku wǝ bakasebi nyɛ lǝ anta eto ɔkuɛ utsyuǝ banlɛ bofee, bǝfǝsǝ anta ǝtsyuǝ balɛ bɔyimi.

ref: Some Pharisees asked Jesus, “Why are your disciples picking grain on the Sabbath? They are not supposed to do that!”
text: Nyaso Farisifɔ bǝtsyuǝ átɔ Yesu bǝnkǝ, “Beso fǝ bakasebi lǝǝyifo nko? Bo kɔfi mǝntǝ kusu bǝ beyifo nko lǝ Ɔnwɛditsyǝyi.”

ref: So the Son of Man is Lord over the Sabbath.”
text: Nyaso mɔ, Utidi Eto Ubii, mɔ ke ni Ɔnwɛditsyǝyi eto Onamute.”

ref: The Pharisees wanted to accuse Jesus of doing something wrong, and they kept watching to see if Jesus would heal him on the Sabbath.
text: Betidi ba lɛkɛ mfó eto bǝtsyuǝ ńyǝ balɛ bobe lǝ wootsya utidi ǝmǝ lǝ ɔnwɛditsyǝyi nǝmǝ ǝsuǝ lǝ babe lǝ buunyǝ etiki lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ.

ref: Jesus told the man to stand up where everyone could see him.
text: Yesu ákpadi utidi ǝmǝ eto kɔni díkpǝ útsyǝ lǝ betidi ba kenke eto ǝnǝmi.

ref: Jesus was angry as he looked around at the people. Yet he felt sorry for them because they were so stubborn. Then he told the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He did, and his bad hand was healed.
text: Yesu étsyi ɔ́wuninsǝ nya óbe mǝ ku lɛblɔfi itsyise ílǝ wǝ lǝ lɛkɔɛsi se bánla bǝ baawunsǝko mǝ bǝtsyuǝ eso. Nya ɔ́tɛyi ufimfi ǝmǝ nkǝ, “Nǝ fǝ kɔni etsyue.” Únǝ ko útsyue. Nya ete kɔni komǝ létsya wǝ.

ref: Jesus decided to ask some of his disciples to go up on a mountain with him, and they went.
text: Nyamfo eto kamaa, Yesu ábe úsu kɔtini disi. Nya mfóo, ókpadi mba óla nkǝ bakɛ ko wǝ.

ref: and to force out demons.
text: Nya biɔkɛ ǝsuǝale bela awɔnɔ bua bitsyiko lǝ betidi ǝsuǝ.”

ref: Some teachers of the Law of Moses came from Jerusalem and said, “This man is under the power of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons! He is even forcing out demons with the help of Beelzebul.”
text: Nya afi eto bateasa ba ditsyi Yerusalem lɛtɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Lɛwɔnɔ bua ǝbǝ disi lǝ Yesu ǝsuǝ. Nya awɔnɔ bua eto ɔtɔnkotɔnkoo, ni nwǝ dítǝ wǝ ǝsuǝale fɛ ɔnlɛ awɔnɔ bua nyamfó bola kabɔnko lǝ betidi ǝsuǝ.”

ref: The crowd sitting around Jesus told him, “Your mother and your brothers and sisters are outside and want to see you.”
text: Nyaso betidi ba létsyilama wǝ átɛyi Yesu bǝnkǝ, “Be, fǝ ambe ku fǝ bayimisani ńyǝ lǝ lekple bǝ bǝ.”

ref: Jesus told the people to gather around him. Then he spoke to them in riddles and said: How can Satan force himself out?
text: Nyaso Yesu ákpadi betidi bamǝ nya údi etiki ko mǝ lǝ lekpa ǝsuǝ nkǝ, “Ntsyǝ yuubǝ bǝ Obonsam ebofo wǝ ǝsuǝ bola ketsyiko lǝ utidi ǝsuǝ?

ref: Jesus went back home, and once again such a large crowd gathered that there was no chance even to eat.
text: Se Yesu dídu mfóo, údu úsu diyo ditsyuǝ. Nya betidi kpǝ esi bǝbǝ mǝ ɔflɔ mfó. Itǝ bǝ ibǝ ɛkɛ ale itǝ Yesu ku wǝ bakasebi bǝ bánkɛ lefe kulaa tsya lǝ bedi lesa.

ref: So if Satan fights against himself, that will be the end of him.
text: Nyaso lǝ Obonsam ataka uńyǝ ɔnlɛ bɔkpɔ ku wǝ ǝsuǝ ku wǝ sekakedikɔ sia lema akpa akpaa, sekakedikɔ siamfó ebusǝnsǝ nya wǝ sekakedidi ebolo.

ref: How can anyone break into the house of a strong man and steal his things, unless he first ties up the strong man? Then he can take everything.
text: “Iyifo etiki eto ɔnukualɛ bǝ utidi ǝmbofo botaka kesu ketu ǝsuǝaletidi lǝ wǝ diyo, ketsyi wǝ bikǝ. Fɛ woofo budufǝ lǝ wǝ diyo nǝmǝǝ, tsyisɛ umuǝ ǝsuǝaletidi ǝmǝ okle.

ref: I promise you that any of the sinful things you say or do can be forgiven, no matter how terrible those things are.
text: “Nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi sitinti minkǝ Onanto ebufi lebua saa nǝ betidi díyifo ku diye bua saa nǝ baakpadi Onanto ɔtsyɛ.

ref: But if you speak against the Holy Spirit, you can never be forgiven. That sin will be held against you forever.
text: Fɛɛ diye bua saa nǝ utidi lookpadi Onanto eto Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛɛ, Onanto ǝmbufi ni ɔtsyɛ wǝ kulefe. Itsyise wuufi lebua nǝmfo fenkǝ lebua nǝ wembotasuǝ.”

ref: Jesus said this because the people were saying that he had an evil spirit in him. Jesus' Mother and Brothers (Matthew
text: Yesu átɛyi nyamfo nko itsyise betidi bǝnkǝ lɛwɔnɔ bua kpe lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ. Bawoe Ni Yesu Eto Batokpenyǝ Tinti?

ref: Jesus' mother and brothers came and stood outside. Then they sent someone with a message for him to come out to them.
text: Lǝ lefe nǝmǝ ǝsuǝǝ, Yesu eto bayimisani ku wǝ ambe ǝbǝ mfó. Bǝńyǝ lǝ lekple nya bákpe wǝ utidi bǝ bahiã wǝ.

ref: Jesus asked, “Who is my mother and who are my brothers?”
text: Nyaso Yesu átɔ mǝ nkǝ, “Owoe ni mɔ ambe? Bawoe ni mɔ bayimisani?”

ref: Then he looked at the people sitting around him and said, “Here are my mother and my brothers.
text: Nya ótsyilamasǝ ǝnǝmi nya óbe betidi ba nsi letsyilama wǝ mfó nya ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Bee, nyǝ mbamfo ni mɔ ambe nye mɔ bayimisani.

ref: Anyone who obeys God is my brother or sister or mother.”
text: Utsyuǝ saa wǝ leeyifo Onanto eto lelabii, wǝ ni mɔ ɔyimisani nye mɔ ɔyimisio, nye mɔ ambe nɛ.”

ref: The next time Jesus taught beside Lake Galilee, a big crowd gathered. It was so large that he had to sit in a boat out on the lake, while the people stood on the shore.
text: Yesu esi ésu Galilea eto ditumbukplɛ flɔ nya ɔnlɛ Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝ mǝ bote. Nya lǝ kase betidi ba létsyilama wǝ mfui esoo, údufǝ lǝ ɔklɔ kafo lǝ ntu bomǝ ǝsuǝ, nya úsiǝ kasɔ. Nya betidi bamǝ díńyǝ lǝ ntu kɛnyɛ fɛ Yesu dídi ko mǝ etiki.

ref: While the farmer was scattering the seed, some of it fell along the road and was eaten by birds.
text: Lǝ lefe nǝ ɔnlɛ eyibibi nyamǝ busǝǝ, íbǝ bǝ ǝtsyuǝ álo ékpo lǝ usunte ɔflɔ, nya baklɔbɛ díbǝ básɛ nnya bámini.

ref: He used stories to teach them many things, and this is part of what he taught:
text: Lefe kpǝ lǝ wǝ asatete ǝsuǝǝ, údi etiki ɔ́yɛnko lǝ akpa ǝsuǝ lǝ ufi ulǝkǝ asa nnya óla nkǝ loote mǝ kasɔ. Asa nnya óte mǝ ni nkǝ.

ref: Now listen! A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
text: “Bɛnɔ! Lefe lɔnii, ɔkuɛmfo ɔni ésu ɔkuɛ nkǝ lǝ usu usiǝ eyibibi ǝtsyuǝ.

ref: Other seeds fell on thin, rocky ground and quickly started growing because the soil wasn't very deep.
text: Ǝtsyuǝ álo ékpo lǝ aba ǝsuǝ, kaka sitǝ lemfui. Nyaso eyibibi nyamfo ǝkǝ mlǝ.

ref: But when the sun came up, the plants were scorched and dried up, because they did not have deep roots.
text: Fɛɛ se kofe lébe kɛnyɛɛ, éblobe nya íkpǝ. Nnya ni bǝ, nnya keyudu mǝnnyǝ didisa itsyi lǝ aba nyamǝ eso.

ref: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked out the plants. So they did not produce any grain.
text: Eyibibi nyamǝ eto ǝtsyuǝ ésu ékpo lǝ biyu nti. Nya kanyi kamǝ léto kéti nnya. Nyaso yámfo bɔnwɔ.

ref: Finally, Jesus said: What is God's kingdom like? What story can I use to explain it?
text: Yesu átɔ nkǝ, “Be muufi nkatesǝ ku Onanto eto sekakedikɔ. Nte lekpa nǝ ele bǝ mfi nlǝkǝ nyamfo eto kasɔ?

ref: When Jesus was alone with the twelve apostles and some others, they asked him about these stories.
text: Lǝ lefe nǝ Yesu mate insii, wǝ bakasebi lefosi inuǝ bamǝ ku mba lǝǝtǝkǝko wǝ ǝbǝ wǝ ɔflɔ nya bátɔ wǝ bǝ ulǝkǝ lekpa nǝmǝ kasɔ ote mǝ.

ref: But they don't have roots, and they don't last very long. As soon as life gets hard or the message gets them in trouble, they give up.
text: Tsya ditiki nǝmǝ eto keyudu ǝnńyǝ kasɔ kukɔnɔ. Baafo kanɔ itǝ lefe kliminti ete. Nya lefe nǝ ke kǝnǝ nye bulǝ sɛkɛ díbǝ lǝ mǝ ǝsuǝ lǝ ditiki nǝmǝ esoo, beesinkli kama.

ref: The reason is, “These people will look and look, but never see. They will listen and listen, but never understand. If they did, they would turn to God and be forgiven.” Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Matthew
text: Nyaso íbǝ itǝ fe kase Onanto eto unyɛlǝkǝtidi lɛwɔni útsyǝ nkǝ, ‘Buukabe tsyaa, bǝǝmbunyǝ bǝtsyǝ lekpo. Buukǝtsyue ato tsyaa, bǝǝmbɔnɔ kasɔ. Nnya ni bǝ lǝ banɔ nye bǝnyǝ tsyaa, bǝǝmbɔnɔ kasɔ. lǝ betsyi mǝ ǝnǝmi bǝtǝ Onanto, bǝ lǝ ufi mǝ abua ɔtsyɛ mǝ.’ ”

ref: The seeds that fell along the road are the people who hear the message. But Satan soon comes and snatches it away from them.
text: Betidi bǝtsyuǝ ale kase eyibibi ǝtsyuǝ luubǝ ekpo lǝ kusu ɔflɔ. Lǝ kase banlɛ bɔnɔɔ, ete Obonsam ǝǝbǝ nya wǝǝnwǝ nnya kǝlǝkǝ lǝ mǝ nsusu ǝsuǝ.

ref: What the farmer is spreading is really the message about the kingdom.
text: Ɔkuɛmfo ǝmǝ lɛ Onanto eto etiki busǝnsǝ.

ref: Jesus also said: You don't light a lamp and put it under a clay pot or under a bed. Don't you put a lamp on a lampstand?
text: Yesu esi ɔ́tɔ mǝ nkǝ, “Utidi aato okandiɛ kefi kakpe lǝ ditsyitsya kalɔ nye lǝ mpa kalɔ? Inni okandiɛ eto kǝtǝkǝkɔ baato okandiɛ kǝtǝkǝ?

ref: Listen carefully to what you hear! The way you treat others will be the way you will be treated—and even worse.
text: Yesu esi ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Bifi mi ato bekpe kukɔnɔ lǝ lesa nǝ bɛnlɛ bɔnɔ ǝsuǝ. Kase faakla asa yeeyi kaba kakpo kǝtǝ utidii, nko ke eto okle Onanto lookla utǝ fintsya nɛ!

ref: Again Jesus said: God's kingdom is like what happens when a farmer scatters seed in a field.
text: Yesu esi ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Onanto eto sekakedikɔ ale osani wǝ laato eyibibi.

ref: It is the ground that makes the seeds sprout and grow into plants that produce grain.
text: Nnya ni bǝnkǝ kasɔ ka báto diyibibi nǝmǝǝ, ke laalɛ ni ola nko. Tutɛ, lǝ díkǝǝ, díabɔ afanto, nya uyi laabɔ. Nya lǝ ébee, yeawɔ ebibi kpǝ.

ref: Then when harvest season comes and the grain is ripe, the farmer cuts it with a sickle. A Mustard Seed (Matthew
text: Lǝ ebibi nyamǝ ábe ɛsɛɛ, ɔkuɛmfo ǝmǝ ǝbǝ kala nnya. Itsyise nnya lɛlɛfe eboyo.” Lekpa Itsyi Lǝ Diyibibi Kɛkɛ Ǝsuǝ.

ref: It is like what happens when a mustard seed is planted in the ground. It is the smallest seed in all the world.
text: Ite fenkǝ utidi éfi diyibibi nǝ nso bɔfɛ lǝ kawunsiǝ úsu óto.

ref: But once it is planted, it grows larger than any garden plant. It even puts out branches that are big enough for birds to nest in its shade. The Reason for Teaching with Stories (Matthew
text: Lefe ditsyuǝ eto kamaa, diǝkǝ kafiani kuyi. Nya nkomǝ eto kuyi komfo laamɔ kaso ɔkuɛ eto ediyi fɛfɛ kenke. Koata kalɛ kpǝ. Nya baklɔbɛ laato ayo lǝ kuyi komǝ eto kalɛ ǝsuǝ.”

ref: He did not tell them anything without using stories. But when he was alone with his disciples, he explained everything to them. A Storm (Matthew
text: Etiki saa wuudi mǝǝ, woatɛyi nnya kayɛ lǝ akpa ǝsuǝ. Lǝ lefe nǝ itǝ kusu bǝ wonko wǝ bakasebi bamǝ hã sii, nya fɛ wǝǝlǝkǝ nnya kasɔ kate mǝ nɛ. Yesu Éti Ufiebi Eto Ǝsuale Kusu.

ref: That evening, Jesus said to his disciples, “Let's cross to the east side.”
text: Diyi nǝmfó eto ditsyitsyo lefee, Yesu átɛyi wǝ bakasebi nkǝ, “Bitǝ lǝ bɔyila ditumbukplɛ nǝmfo busu ni lekpake nǝ nse.”

ref: Jesus used many other stories when he spoke to the people, and he taught them as much as they could understand.
text: Yesu édi mǝ etiki nyamfo lǝ kase utsyuǝ sa loofo kasɔ bɔnɔ.

ref: So they left the crowd, and his disciples started across the lake with him in the boat. Some other boats followed along.
text: Nyaso bédu lǝ betidi ǝto ditsyukpa nǝmfo ɔflɔ bésu bedufǝ bǝsiǝ lǝ ɔklɔ wǝ Yesu dísiǝ ǝsuǝ, nya bétsyiko ditumbukplɛ nǝmǝ bɔyila. Kaklɔ bamba tsya ǝńyǝ mfó.

ref: Suddenly a storm struck the lake. Waves started splashing into the boat, and it was about to sink.
text: Yanklɛ fɛ ufiebi ko dítsyiko bɔsɔ bembembe nya ɔ́sɔ ntu ókpo lǝ ɔklɔ ǝmǝ kafo, nya ibu sɛkɛ lǝ ntu lǝ buyi ɔklɔ ǝmǝ lǝ onya udu fǝ lǝ ntu kasɔ.

ref: Jesus was in the back of the boat with his head on a pillow, and he was asleep. His disciples woke him and said, “Teacher, don't you care that we're about to drown?”
text: Lǝ lefe nǝmfóo, Yesu ǝsiǝ lǝ ɔklɔ ǝmǝ eto kama, úfi wǝ disi útǝkǝ lǝ esunde ǝsuǝ nya ɔnlɛ selabe. Nyaso básanklisǝ wǝ nya bátɛyi wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Oteasa, ɛnlɛ fǝ bulǝ bǝnkǝ boǝbǝ bukpǝ?”

ref: Jesus got up and ordered the wind and the waves to be quiet. The wind stopped, and everything was calm.
text: Yesu átaka úńyǝ nya ófa ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Ufiebi, yɛ bɔsɔ!” Nya ɔ́tɛyi ntu bomǝ tsya nkǝ, “Ntu yɛ bonwa!” Nya ufiebi ǝmǝ disinkli ɔ́yɛ bɔsɔ, nya ntu bomǝ eto ǝnǝmi disinkli búyuǝ buɛɛ kananaa.

ref: Jesus asked his disciples, “Why were you afraid? Don't you have any faith?”
text: Nya Yesu lɛtɔ wǝ bakasebi nkǝ, “Beso biate sikpi mmǝ? Bíamfo tɔ bɛnɔ?”

ref: Now they were more afraid than ever and said to each other, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him!”
text: Sikpi ǝmuǝ mǝ sefensǝ, nya bétsyiko mǝ bǝtsyuǝ bɔtɔ bǝnkǝ, “Owoe ko ni utidi ǝmfo? Bǝ ntu bomǝ ku ufiebi kenke etsyue wǝ etiki koto!”

ref: She had said to herself, “If I can just touch his clothes, I will be healed.”
text: Ɔ́tɛyi wǝ ǝsuǝ nkǝ, “Lǝ mfó wǝ awu butidi koo, eni bufi bomǝ ǝbɔyɛ mɛ.”

ref: Jesus said to the woman, “You are now well because of your faith. May God give you peace! You are healed, and you will no longer be in pain.”
text: Yesu átɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Mɔ ubisiobi, fǝ bofokanɔ átsya fǝ. Su lǝ diyuǝ ǝsuǝ. Fǝ kanyi ensiǝmbu sinkli kǝbǝ bio.”

ref: She had gone to many doctors, and they had not done anything except cause her a lot of pain. She had paid them all the money she had. But instead of getting better, she only got worse.
text: Ɔkɛ lǝ kanyi ǝsuǝ lǝ lefe nǝ eto lefe mba leeyifo afa díkǝtǝ wǝ afa kulaatsya. Wóbiasǝ wǝ atabi kenke ótsya bufi bomfo, tsya bufi bomǝ mányuǝ wǝ. Kato bomu bóbe.

ref: Then Jesus went inside and said to them, “Why are you crying and carrying on like this? The child isn't dead. She is just asleep.”
text: údufǝ lǝ mǝnti nya ɔ́tɔ mǝ nkǝ, “Be léboko kudu komfo bokpo? Beso bɛnlɛ nku? Dikpefi nǝmfó ménkpǝ. Selabe ete onlabe.”

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed Lake Galilee and came to shore near the town of Gerasa.
text: Nyamfo eto kamaa, Yesu ku wǝ bakasebi édu bésu Galilea eto Ditumbukplɛ eto situma sia nse lǝ Gerasefɔ eto kasɔ.

ref: from the graveyard where he had been living. No one was able to tie the man up anymore, not even with a chain.
text: Itsyise mfó úkǝsiǝ nɛ. Lǝ lefe nǝ lefe mmǝǝ, betidi ba kenke mámfo wǝ bumuǝ kakpe lǝ amana.

ref: He had often been put in chains and leg irons, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one could control him.
text: Lǝ lefe kpǝ ǝsuǝǝ, bákafo wǝ ani ku akpa bokle. Fɛɛ wókala nnya bufǝsǝ kabɔ. Utidi ǝmfo akɛ ǝsuǝale tinti, itǝ bǝ kutsyuǝ saa mánkamfo wǝ bumuǝ kǝtsyǝ.

ref: Night and day he was in the graveyard or on the hills, yelling and cutting himself with stones.
text: Kǝtsyǝ ku kakɔmi kenke úkatsyilama lǝ betidi eto edi ku atini nyamǝ nti, nya úkakpo kudu, ékefi abambi katsya wǝ ǝsuǝ.

ref: When the man saw Jesus in the distance, he ran up to him and knelt down.
text: Utidi ǝmfo ǝńyǝ kɔsɛsɛ fɛ únyǝ Yesu. Nyaso útsyetsyi úsu óse akonki lǝ Yesu eto ǝnǝmi.

ref: He shouted, “Jesus, Son of God in heaven, what do you want with me? Promise me in God's name that you won't torture me!”
text: Nya ófa bembembe ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Onanto Fanlonlo eto Ubi! Be nkpe lǝ mɔ ku fǝ lenti mfo? Nlɛ fǝ lekpakpa, se mɛ uyi lǝ Onanto eto diye ǝsuǝ fǝnkǝ embunǝ mɛ koto!”

ref: He then begged Jesus not to send them away.
text: Nya ɔ́lɛ Yesu lekpakpa nkǝ woanla mǝ lǝ kasɔ kamǝ.

ref: Over on the hillside a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: Lǝ lefe nǝmfó ǝsuǝǝ, bakplako kpǝ ǝńyǝ lɛ kɔtini komǝ ɔflɔ balɛ budi.

ref: So the evil spirits begged Jesus, “Send us into those pigs! Let us go into them.”
text: Nya awɔnɔ nyamfo lɛlɛ Yesu lekpakpa bǝnkǝ, “La bo akpe lǝ bakplako ba ńyǝ lǝ mmǝ, lǝ budu budufǝ lǝ mǝ ǝsuǝ.”

ref: The men taking care of the pigs ran to the town and the farms to spread the news. Then the people came out to see what had happened.
text: Nya mba nlɛ bakplako bamǝ bobee, dítsyetsyi bésu umǝ ǝmǝ ku mba lɛkɛ lǝ wǝ ɔflɔ mfó, nya bésu batɛyi betidi ba kenke lesa nǝ díbǝ. Mba bátɛyi ésu mfó, bésu bábe lesa nǝ díbǝ.

ref: When they came to Jesus, they saw the man who had once been full of demons. He was sitting there with his clothes on and in his right mind, and they were terrified.
text: Nya lefe nǝ bǝbǝ Yesu ɔflɔɔ, bǝnyǝ utidi wǝ awɔnɔ bua kpǝ dísiǝ lǝ ǝsuǝ mfó. Nya únsi lǝ kasɔ, ónkpe awu, íkakpa ko wǝ. Nya sikpi dímuǝ mǝmblɛ kenke.

ref: Everyone who had seen what had happened told about the man and the pigs.
text: Betidi ba leka wǝ búnyǝǝ, átɛyi lesa saa nǝ díbǝ lǝ utidi ǝmǝ ku bakplako bamǝ ǝsuǝ.

ref: Then the people started begging Jesus to leave their part of the country.
text: Nyaso bálɛ Yesu lekpakpa bǝ udu lǝ mǝ kasɔ.

ref: But Jesus would not let him. Instead, he said, “Go home to your family and tell them how much the Lord has done for you and how good he has been to you.”
text: Fɛɛ Yesu mántɔnɔ. Bomu ɔ́tɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Sinkli esu fǝ betidi ɔflɔ lǝ atɛyi mǝ kase Onamute ǝmǝ lɛwunsǝ ko fǝ ku kase únyǝ fǝ kaminsǝ.”

ref: The man went away into the region near the ten cities known as Decapolis and began telling everyone how much Jesus had done for him. Everyone who heard what had happened was amazed. A Dying Girl and a Sick Woman (Matthew
text: Nyaso osani ǝmfo édu úsu ótsyilama Kǝmǝ Lefosi kamǝ nya ɔ́tɛyi betidi bamǝ lesa saa nǝ Yesu díyifo útǝ wǝ. Nya íyifo mba lɛnɔ nnya yanii. Yesu Átsya Yairo Wǝ Ndi Yudafɔ Eto Katsyakɔ Eto Uninǝ Eto Ubisiobi Nya Ótsya Usio Fimfi Utsyuǝ Tsya.

ref: But the people laughed at him. After Jesus had sent them all out of the house, he took the girl's father and mother and his three disciples and went to where she was.
text: Se ɔ́tɛyi nkoo, betidi ba ńyǝ mfó étsyiko wǝ bumǝ. Nyaso Yesu ǝtǝ nkǝ mǝmblɛ bɔ besu lekple, nya útǝ ubi ǝmǝ eto anto ku ambe ku wǝ bakasebi itsyǝ bamǝ nkǝ bedufǝ kaka ubi ǝmǝ díkpe ónlabe lǝ kasɔ.

ref: Once again Jesus got into the boat and crossed Lake Galilee. Then as he stood on the shore, a large crowd gathered around him.
text: Yesu ǝsiǝ lǝ ɔklɔ úsinkli úsu ditumbukplɛ nǝmǝ eto Yudafɔ eto lekpake nǝ nse. Se úsu údufǝ ditumbukplɛ nǝmǝ eto kɛnyɛ mfóo, betidi kpǝ ǝbǝ bátsyilama wǝ.

ref: The person in charge of the synagogue was also there. His name was Jairus, and when he saw Jesus, he went over to him. He knelt at Jesus' feet
text: Nya Yudafɔ eto katsyakɔ eto uninǝ ɔni utsyuǝ wǝ baakpo Yairo ǝbǝ mfó. Se únyǝ Yesuu, óse akonki lǝ wǝ akɔ.

ref: and started begging him for help. He said, “My little daughter is about to die! Please come and touch her, so she will get well and live.”
text: Nya ɔ́lɛ wǝ lekpakpa kekleke nkǝ, “Mɔ ubisiobi kɛkɛ lɛ bufi kekleke. Nyaso bǝ lǝ ǝbǝ efi fǝ kɔni ǝtǝkǝ lǝ wǝ bǝ lǝ ǝsuǝ lǝ imuǝ wǝ ale lǝ ɔkɛ nkpǝ.”

ref: Jesus went with Jairus. Many people followed along and kept crowding around.
text: Nyaso Yesu átaka útǝkǝko wǝ. Nya lǝ kase betidi ba díkǝko Yesu difui esoo, bátsyilama wǝ se bántɔ.

ref: In the crowd was a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years.
text: Nya usio utsyuǝ akɛ lǝ mǝ nti, wǝ kɔtɔ díkakpo lǝ lefosi alɛ ǝnuǝ sɔ̃ɔ̃ ǝsuǝ.

ref: The woman had heard about Jesus, so she came up behind him in the crowd and barely touched his clothes.
text: Ɔ́nɔ etiki itsyi lǝ Yesu ǝsuǝ, nyaso útǝkǝko betidi ba dítǝkǝko Yesu kama.

ref: At that moment Jesus felt power go out from him. He turned to the crowd and asked, “Who touched my clothes?”
text: Lamfolamfo Yesu áte nkǝ ǝsuǝale ǝtsyuǝ ábɔ wǝ lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ. Nyaso úkǝnǝ úńyǝ, nya ɔ́tsyi ɔwuninsǝ, nya ɔ́tɔ betidi bamǝ ntǝkǝko wǝ nkǝ, “Owoe ni futsyǝ dítidi mɛ awu?”

ref: His disciples said to him, “Look at all these people crowding around you! How can you ask who touched you?”
text: Wǝ bakasebi bamǝ átɔ wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Be kase betidi bamfo letsyilama fǝ. Beso faatɔ fǝ, owoe ni futsyǝ dítidi fǝ?”

ref: But Jesus turned to see who had touched him.
text: Tsyaa, Yesu esi úkǝnǝ úńyǝ ɔlɛ ǝnǝmi botsyilamasǝ kabe lǝ woofo futsyǝ dítidi wǝ bunyǝ.

ref: The woman knew what had happened to her. So she came trembling with fear and knelt down in front of Jesus. Then she told him the whole story.
text: Usio ǝmǝ mfó ǝnyǝ utsyǝ lekpo nkǝ lesatsyuǝ ǝbǝ wǝ lǝ ǝsuǝ. Nyaso lǝ sikpitete ku ketinkǝ ǝsuǝǝ, ɔ́bɔ úbǝ óse akonki lǝ Yesu eto akɔ. Nya ɔ́tɛyi wǝ lǝ ɔnukualɛ ǝsuǝ, lesa nǝ díbǝ.

ref: While Jesus was still speaking, some people came from Jairus' home and said, “Your daughter has died! Why bother the teacher anymore?”
text: Lǝ lefe nǝ Yesu nlɛ nyamfo bɔtɛyii, batɔnkpe bǝtsyuǝ étsyi Yudafɔ eto katsyakɔ kamǝ eto uninǝ ǝmǝ eto diyo bǝbǝ nya bátɛyi wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Fǝ ubi ǝmǝ ébukpǝ koko. Beso fensi fanlɛ Oteasa ǝmfo bulǝ?”

ref: Jesus heard what they said, and he said to Jairus, “Don't worry. Just have faith!”
text: Yesu méntsyue ditiki nǝ mba nlɛ bɔtɛyi mfó ato. Bomu ɔ́tɛyi Yairo nkǝ, “Mante sikpi, fo anɔ ete.”

ref: Jesus did not let anyone go with him except Peter and the two brothers, James and John.
text: Nya Yesu lékpa Petro ku Yakobo ku wǝ ɔyimisani Yohanes mate hã, nya bésu Yairo eto diyo.

ref: They went home with Jairus and saw the people crying and making a lot of noise.
text: Nya lefe nǝ bédufǝ diyo nǝmǝ, nya Yesu dínyǝ kase banlɛ kanyi bunyǝ kakpo edu kewi nkuu,

ref: He took the twelve-year-old girl by the hand and said, “Talitha, koum!” which means, “Little girl, get up!” The girl got right up and started walking around. Everyone was greatly surprised.
text: Lamfolamfo, disiobi nǝmǝ nǝ dikafo lefosi alɛ ǝnuǝǝ, átaka úńyǝ nya útsyiko bɔyɛ katsyilama. Se íbǝ nkoo, íyifo mǝmblɛ yanii.

ref: But Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone what had happened. Then he said, “Give her something to eat.”
text: Nyaso Yesu ǝkǝ mǝ ato kekleke nkǝ bantɛyi kuutsyuǝ saa. Nya kamaa, ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Bitǝ wǝ lesatsyuǝ lǝ udi.”

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed the lake and brought the boat to shore near the town of Gennesaret.
text: Se bédufǝ ditumbukplɛ nǝmǝ eto lekpake nǝ nsee, báyifǝ lǝ Genesaret eto kasɔ. Nya bátsyasǝ mǝ ɔklɔ ǝmǝ mfó.

ref: But Jesus said, “Prophets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their relatives and their own family.”
text: Yesu átɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Onanto eto unyǝlǝkǝtidi kpe dibu lǝ nlebe kpǝ. Fɛɛ lǝ wǝ kosate eto umǝǝ, lǝ wǝ betidi ntii, ku lǝ wǝ kosate eto diyo eto betidi eto ǝnǝmii, umbǝ dibu saa.”

ref: But when Herod heard about Jesus, he said, “This must be John! I had his head cut off, and now he has come back to life.”
text: Lefe nǝ Herodes lɛnɔ nyamfóo, wuntsya átɛyi nkǝ, “Yohanes wǝ útǝ bála disi uni disi ósankli utsyi ɔkɔmǝ úbǝ!”

ref: After the apostles returned to Jesus, they told him everything they had done and taught.
text: Batɔnkpe ba Yesu lékpe nkǝ besu batsyilama lekpake mfóo, ésinkli bǝbǝ bátɛyi wǝ nnya kenke bésu beyifo ku asa nnya báte.

ref: The next Sabbath he taught in the synagogue. Many of the people who heard him were amazed and asked, “How can he do all this? Where did he get such wisdom and the power to work these miracles?
text: Nya lǝ Yudafɔ eto Ɔnwɛditsyǝyi ǝsuǝǝ, úsu ɔlɛ Anantotiki búdi lǝ Yudafɔ eto katsyakɔ mfó. Nyaso íyifo betidi ba kenke lɛnɔ wǝ asatetee yanii. Bátɔ bǝtsyuǝ bǝnkǝ, “Sieti ókase etiki nyamfo eto okle utsyiko? Nte kobe ko eto okle nkpe wǝ bǝ woafo ayanisa nyamfo eto okle buyifo?

ref: Isn't he the carpenter, the son of Mary? Aren't James, Joseph, Judas, and Simon his brothers? Don't his sisters still live here in our town?” The people were upset because of what he was doing.
text: Inni wǝ ni Maria eto ubi wǝ ni nwǝ laawe keyi? Lee inni wǝ ni Yakobo ku Yose ku Yuda ku Simon eto ɔyimisani? Nya wǝ bayimisio tsya nkpe mfo!” Nyaso betidi bamfo mámfo wǝ.

ref: He told them, “You may take along a walking stick. But don't carry food or a traveling bag or any money.
text: Nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Mi kuutsyuǝ saa manlɛ kulesa usuko kusu komǝ, enso uyi wǝ baalɛɛ kɔni hã. Bientsyi didisa nye otobe fenkǝ mba laatɔ asa kafe. Bientsyi atabi lǝ mi katobe.

ref: If any place won't welcome you or listen to your message, leave and shake the dust from your feet as a warning to them.”
text: Lǝ bídufǝ umǝ utsyuǝ nya betidi bamfó dímamfo mi nye báni bǝ beembutsyue mi ditiki kotoo, bidu lǝ bɛflɛ mi nkpafo eto sitǝ bekpo mfó. Nyamfo loote Onanto eto kotonǝnǝ eto ditsyǝbi nǝ lǝǝbǝ bubǝ itǝ mǝ lǝ umǝ ǝmǝ.”

ref: Herodias had a grudge against John and wanted to kill him. But she could not do it
text: Nyaso Yohanes eto etiki ǝkǝlǝ Herodia, nyaso úkǝkisi wǝ. Nya ɔ́bɛbɛ kusu nkǝ lǝ olo wǝ.

ref: because Herod was afraid of John and protected him. He knew that John was a good and holy man. Even though Herod was confused by what John said, he was glad to listen to him. And he often did.
text: Tsyaa, wámfo kusu bunyǝ lǝ Herodes eso. Itsyise Herodes áte Yohanes sikpi se unyi nkǝ Yohanes éyifo ɔnukualɛtidi ku utidi wǝ díyifo kɛnkɛ eso. Nyaso úkase wǝ. Lefe kpǝ, óla nkǝ lǝ úkanɔ wǝ asatete, ite bǝ wǝ etiki nyamǝ káto wǝ lǝ lɛkɔɛsi lɛ ɔ́nɔ nnya tsya fɛ.

ref: I swear that I will give you as much as half of my kingdom, if you want it.”
text: Nya usi ɔ́tɛyi wǝ tsya nkǝ, “Ekpe lǝ mɔ lekankpomɛ, minkǝ muutǝ fǝ lesa nǝ ke fɔɔtɔ mɛ, lǝ mɔ sekakedikɔ eto kutsyǝ kulaa tsya!”

ref: The girl left and asked her mother, “What do you think I should ask for?” Her mother answered, “The head of John the Baptist!”
text: Nyaso usiobi ǝmfo átaka úsu ɔ́tɔ wǝ ambe nkǝ, “Be ele bǝ ntɔ?” Nya wǝ ambe lɛtɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Tɔ wǝ Yohanes Ntuflǝsǝtidi eto disi.”

ref: The girl hurried back and told Herod, “Here and now on a serving plate I want the head of John the Baptist!”
text: Disiobi nǝmfo ésinkli kpla dísu diyo nǝmǝ kafo nya ɔ́tɛyi Herodes nkǝ, “Nla minkǝ la Yohanes Ntuflǝsǝtidi eto disi ǝtǝkǝ lǝ ɔkpɛ tɛnklɛ ǝsuǝ ǝtǝ mɛ mɔmɔmɔmɔ!”

ref: Herod was very sorry for what he had said. But he did not want to break the promise he had made in front of his guests.
text: Nyamfo ába oka Herodes lɛkɔɛsi, fɛɛ wǝnsiǝmbofo ntamu bo óka utsyǝ lǝ beninǝtidi bamfó eto ǝnǝmi butu.

ref: The guard put the head on a serving plate and took it to the girl. Then she gave it to her mother.
text: Kamaa, úfi disi nǝmǝ útǝkǝ lǝ ɔkpɛ tɛnklɛ ǝsuǝ nya úfi ni oboko disiobi nǝmǝ. Nya wuntsya dífi ni úsu útǝ wǝ ambe.

ref: When John's followers learned that he had been killed, they took his body and put it in a tomb. Jesus Feeds Five Thousand (Matthew
text: Lefe nǝ Yohanes eto bakasebi lɛnɔ ditiki nǝmfoo, bésu báfo wǝ ɔkɔ bésu bébikǝ. Yesu Ǝtǝ Betidi Akpi Anɔ Lesa Bédi.

ref: But so many people were coming and going that Jesus and the apostles did not even have a chance to eat. Then Jesus said, “Let's go to a place where we can be alone and get some rest.”
text: Nya kaka mǝ ku Yesu lɛsɔnɔ eto betidi kpǝ ba nlɛ búbǝ kédu étsyiko bufui. Nyaso Yesu ku wǝ bakasebi mánkɛ lefe lǝ bedi lesa kula tsya. Nyaso Yesu átɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Bitǝ lǝ bo hã lǝ busu kaka lɛbiɔ lǝ busiǝ mfó butsyǝ ɔnwɛ sɛkɛ.”

ref: They left in a boat for a place where they could be alone.
text: Nyaso bésu bédufǝ lǝ ɔklɔ lǝ besu kaka betidi lǝmbǝ.

ref: That evening the disciples came to Jesus and said, “This place is like a desert, and it's already late.
text: Lefe nǝ ditsyitsyo léyoo, wǝ bakasebi ányi bésu wǝ ɔflɔ nya bátɛyi wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Kale bolo. Nya kase mfo diyifo ntɛntii, bǝǝmbunyǝ kulesa bedi.

ref: Jesus replied, “You give them something to eat.” But they asked him, “Don't you know it would take almost a year's wages to buy all of these people something to eat?”
text: Yesu átɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Mi kosatee, bitǝ mǝ lesatsyuǝ lǝ bedi.” Nya bátɛyi wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Nyamfó ebofo fe utsyuǝ eto afa mflamfla eto kayofofo! Ala fǝ bobiasǝ nyamfo lǝ mǝ budi ǝsuǝ?”

ref: Then Jesus said, “How much bread do you have? Go and see!” They found out and answered, “We have five small loaves of bread and two fish.”
text: Nya Yesu lɛtɔ mǝ nkǝ, “Abolo asia bɛnlɛ? Bisu bebe!” Nya se bésu bábee, bátɛyi wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Abolo anɔ ku akpɛ ǝnuǝ ete ntǝ.”

ref: Jesus told his disciples to tell the people to sit down on the green grass.
text: Yesu átɛyi wǝ bakasebi nkǝ bǝtǝ lǝ betidi bamǝ kenke lǝ bǝsiǝ kasɔ lǝ sefa siamǝ ǝsuǝ mfó lǝ etsyukpa etsyukpa ǝsuǝ.

ref: All of them saw him and were terrified. But at this same time he said, “Don't worry! I am Jesus. Don't be afraid.”
text: Mǝmblɛ áte sikpi se bǝnyǝ wǝ. Nyaso Yesu átɛyi mǝ lamfolamfo nkǝ, “Biante sikpi! Mɔ uni! Bimuǝ utu bɛlɛ!”

ref: Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples to give to the people. He also divided the two fish, so everyone could have some.
text: Nya Yesu dífi abolo ku akpɛ nyamǝ, nya se óbe kato, nya ɔ́sɔ Onanto ani. Nya úbudi abolo nyamǝ nya úfi nnya útǝ wǝ bakasebi nkǝ bǝsiǝ bǝtǝ etsyukpa etsyukpa nyamǝ. Nya úsiǝ akpɛ nyamɛ tsya nko útǝ mǝ.

ref: After everyone had eaten all they wanted,
text: Mba kenke lɛkɛ mfó édi bémi.

ref: Jesus' disciples picked up twelve large baskets of leftover bread and fish.
text: Nya se wǝ bakasebi lɛsɛ didisa nǝ díbu bakɔɔ, díyi lefosi etsyitsya kplɛ ǝnuǝ.

ref: At once, Jesus made his disciples get into the boat and start back across to Bethsaida. But he stayed until he had sent the crowds away.
text: Lamfolamfo Yesu átɛyi wǝ bakasebi nkǝ bedufǝ lǝ ɔklɔ ǝmǝ lǝ besu umǝ wǝ baakpo Betsaida, lǝ ditumbukplɛ nǝmǝ eto lekpake nǝ nse. Nya útǝ nkǝ sitiditu siamǝ sǝnsǝ sidu.

ref: Then he told them goodbye and went up on the side of a mountain to pray.
text: Nyamfo eto kamaa, wuntsya édu óbe kɔtini komǝ nkǝ lǝ usu oto ola.

ref: Later in the evening he was still there by himself, and the boat was somewhere in the middle of the lake.
text: Se éyo ketsyee, ɔklɔ ǝmǝ áyo ditumbukplɛ nǝmǝ eto ntinti, Yesu mate hã díbu uńyǝ mfó.

ref: He could see that the disciples were struggling hard, because they were rowing against the wind. Not long before morning, Jesus came toward them. He was walking on the water and was about to pass the boat.
text: Únyǝ nkǝ wǝ betidi édufǝ lǝ bulǝ ǝsuǝ ku kase bánlɛ ɔklɔ ǝmǝ bofo bonwa itsyise ufiebi lɛ bɔsɔ kǝlǝ mǝ. Nyaso lǝ kolensiǝnto kplɛɛ, Yesu áyɛ lǝ ntu bomǝ ǝsuǝ úsu mǝ ɔflɔ eti nkǝ lǝ ɔyɛ ofe.

ref: When the disciples saw Jesus walking on the water, they thought he was a ghost, and they started screaming.
text: Nya se bǝnyǝ wǝ lǝ ntu bomǝ ǝsuǝǝ, báfa bátɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Ɔkɔ ni.”

ref: He then got into the boat with them, and the wind died down. The disciples were completely confused.
text: Nya óbe údufǝ lǝ mǝ ɔklɔ ǝmǝ kafo, nya ete ufiebi ǝmǝ lɛyɛ bɔsɔ. Nyamfo éyifo wǝ bakasebi yanii ku kafɔsu.

ref: Their minds were closed, and they could not understand the true meaning of the loaves of bread. Jesus Heals Sick People in Gennesaret (Matthew
text: Itsyise bánte kase ko íbǝ fɛ útǝ betidi akpi anɔ bamfo lesa bédi banɔ. Mǝ nsusu mánkpa ko mǝ lǝ nyamfó ǝsuǝ, itsyise bámfo wǝ bánɔ. Yesu Átsya Befimfi Lǝ Genesaret Eto Kasɔ.

ref: As soon as they got out of the boat, the people recognized Jesus.
text: Kase báyifǝ lǝ ɔklɔ ǝmǝ koo, betidi ǝtsyǝ Yesu lekpo.

ref: So they ran all over that part of the country to bring their sick people to him on mats. They brought them each time they heard where he was.
text: Nyaso betidi bamǝ étsyetsyi bésu bátɛyi mǝ betidi kenke lǝ kasɔ kamǝ. Nya kaka ke bánɔ bǝ Yesu kpee, bétsyi befimfi lǝ mǝ belabekǝ ǝsuǝ báboko wǝ ɔflɔ.

ref: In every village or farm or marketplace where Jesus went, the people brought their sick to him. They begged him to let them just touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
text: Nya kaka ke Yesu dísu koo, umǝ ni o-o, kebuto keni o-o, betidi ko díketsyi mǝ befimfi kaboko obia nya bánlɛ wǝ lekpakpa bǝnkǝ utǝ lǝ betidi wǝ awu eto atosi ete. Nya mba dítidi nnyaa, ǝmuǝ ale.

ref: Some Pharisees and several teachers of the Law of Moses from Jerusalem came and gathered around Jesus.
text: Farisifɔ ku afi eto bateasa ba dítsyi Yerusalem ǝbǝ bátsyilama Yesu.

ref: You are good at rejecting God's commands so that you can follow your own teachings!
text: Yesu esi ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Bekpe awɛsu ǝtsyuǝ nnya biefi akayɛ lǝ nnya ǝsuǝ kani Onanto eto kɔfi budi, keyifo batekoko ku banakoko eto akpa.

ref: They noticed that some of his disciples ate without first washing their hands.
text: Bǝnyǝ bǝ wǝ bakasebi si balɛ lesa budi nya bǝtsyuǝ mǝmflǝ mǝ ani fe kase Farisifɔ laate betidi bǝ ele bǝ bǝflǝ.

ref: The Pharisees and many others obey the teachings of their ancestors. They always wash their hands in the proper way before eating.
text: Bǝnyǝ nyamfo bǝtsyǝ lekpo itsyise Farisifɔ ku Yudafɔ fɛfɛ kenke eeyifo lǝ mǝ batekoko, banakoko eto akpa nnya báyani bǝtsyǝ nya báte mǝ ǝsuǝ.

ref: None of them will eat anything they buy in the market until it is washed. They also follow a lot of other teachings, such as washing cups, pitchers, and bowls.
text: Nya lǝ báya lesa saa betsyiko bikǝkatekɔ tsyaa, ele bǝ bǝflǝ mǝ ani fɛ lǝ bedi. Nya ele bǝ beyifo lǝ afi kpǝ nnya bǝbǝ bétu, fe kase bǝǝflǝ akɔbe ku ekplibi ku kalɔtsyi.

ref: The Pharisees and teachers asked Jesus, “Why don't your disciples obey what our ancestors taught us to do? Why do they eat without washing their hands?”
text: Lǝ nyaso Farisifɔ ku afi eto bateasa átɔ Yesu bǝnkǝ, “Beso fǝ mba ntǝkǝko fǝ lǝǝnyifo lǝ bo batekoko ku banakoko eto akpa ǝsuǝ? Nya banlɛ asa budi lǝ mǝ ani mǝflǝmflǝmflǝ ǝsuǝ?”

ref: Jesus replied: You are nothing but show-offs! The prophet Isaiah was right when he wrote that God had said, “All of you praise me with your words, but you never really think about me.
text: Yesu ǝlǝkǝ kɛnyɛ ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Ǝnǝmi ǝnǝmi eto betidi bini! Lesa nǝ Onanto eto Unyɛlǝkǝtidi Yesaya lɛwɔni ɔ́tɛyi utsyǝ lǝ mi ǝsuǝǝ, íbǝ ítǝ sitinti! Ɔ́tɛyi bo nkǝ, Onanto átɛyi nkǝ, ‘Betidi bamfoo, ǝnǝmi ǝnǝmi eto dibu ete bǝǝtǝ mɛ, banla mɛ itsyi lǝ mǝ akɔɛsi.

ref: It is useless for you to worship me, when you teach rules made up by humans.”
text: Bimba ete ini bǝ bálɛ mɔ lelabi buyifo. Baate kawunsiǝ eto afi nnya betidi dítsyǝ fenkǝ mɔ afi ini.’

ref: You disobey God's commands in order to obey what humans have taught.
text: Nnya lete bǝ bifi mɔ kɔfi bɛkula nya bɛnlɛ mi kawunsiǝ mfo eto akpa boté.”

ref: But you let people get by without helping their parents when they should. You let them say that what they own has been offered to God.
text: Fɛɛ biatɛyi biǝnkǝ ɛlɛkɛ bǝ utidi ebofo lesa bɔni butǝ wǝ anto ku ambe ba lesa lehiã, ku nsusu bǝ lesa nǝmǝ eyifo kɛnkɛ, kato taflatsyǝ ku nsusu bǝ wuufi lesa nǝmǝ utǝ Onanto bomu eso.

ref: You won't let those people help their parents.
text: Nya ɛyɛ le bo akpa ǝsuǝǝ, boata Onanto eto kɔfi kebu.
